data.description: An aboleth with this feat can extend its mucus cloud into a wider area.
data.benefit: The aboleth's mucus cloud extends to fill an area equal to its natural reach (a 10-foot radius for most aboleths).
data.normal: The aboleth's mucus cloud has a radius of only 1 foot.
data.special: On land, the aboleth's mucus cloud becomes a mucus puddle of an equal radius. The mucus can affect other beings in contact with the puddle (including any that move through it using their base land speed), but beings that jump or fly over it, or otherwise avoid the puddle, are safe.

data.name: Enlarge Mystery
data.description: You can cast mysteries farther than normal.
data.benefit: Once per day, you can alter a mystery with a range of close, medium, or long to increase its range by 100%. This functions in most respects as the Enlarge Spell feat.
data.special: You can take this feat multiple times.

data.name: Enlarge Power
data.description: You can manifest powers farther than normal.
data.benefit: To use this feat, you must expend your psionic focus (see Concentration skill description). You can alter a power with a range of close, medium, or long to increase its range by 100%. An enlarged power with a range of close has a range of 50 feet + 5 feet per level, a medium-range power has a range of 200 feet + 20 feet per level, and a long-range power has a range of 800 feet + 80 feet per level. Powers whose ranges are not defined by distance, as well as powers whose ranges are not close, medium, or long, are not affected.Using this feat does not increase the power point cost of the power.

data.name: Enlarge Spell
data.description: You can cast spells farther than normal.
data.benefit: An enlarged spell has its range doubled. Spells whose ranges are not defined by distance do not have their ranges increased. A spell whose area or effect is determined by its range (such as bless or a cone spell) has the dimensions of its area or effect increased proportionally. An enlarged spell uses up a spell slot one level higher than the spell's actual level.

data.name: Enlarge Supernatural Ability
data.description: You can increase the range of a supernatural attack.
data.benefit: Once per day, you can enlarge a supernatural ability. When you use an enlarged supernatural ability, its range increases by 100%. You must declare use of this feat before you use the ability. A continuous use ability (such as a gaze attack) can only be enlarged for 1 round. Enlarging a supernatural ability does not require a separate action.This feat does not affect the length of a line or a cone, the diameter of an emanation (such as a gaze attack or frightful presence), or the range of any supernatural ability not defined by distance.
data.special: This feat can be taken multiple times. Each time it is taken, you can enlarge a supernatural ability one additional time per day. You cannot enlarge a particular use of a supernatural ability more than once.

data.name: Enlarge Utterance
data.description: You can project the power of an utterance to a greater distance
data.prerequisite: Truespeak 6 ranks, ability to speak utterances
data.benefit: If the utterance is successful, you can increase its range by 100%. For most utterances, this means the range becomes 120 feet. This feat has no effect on utterances with a range of touch. An enlarged utterance has a Truespeak DC 5 higher than normal.

data.name: Enspell Familiar
data.description: You can cast spells on your familiar over a distance.
data.prerequisite: Caster level 1st, ability to gain a familiar.
data.benefit: You are always considered to be in contact your familiar for the purpose of casting shared spells. Thus any spell you cast on yourself also affects your familiar, as long as it is within mile of you.
data.normal: The familiar must be within 5 feet of the caster at the time such a spell is cast.

data.name: Entangling Exhalation
data.description: You can use your breath weapon to create an entangling mesh of energy.
data.benefit: When you use your breath weapon, you can choose to enmesh all creatures in its area instead of producing its normal effect. Your breath weapon deals only half its normal damage; however, any creature that takes damage from your breath weapon becomes entangled and takes an extra 1d6 points of damage, of the same energy type as normally dealt by your breath weapon, each round at the start of your turn. This effect lasts for 1d4 rounds.If your breath weapon doesn't deal energy damage, creatures damaged by the initial breath are still entangled but don't take additional damage on later rounds.

data.name: Entangling Spell
data.description: Your spell releases residual eldritch power that entangles your enemies.
data.benefit: The feat can be applied to any instantaneous spell that deals acid, cold, electricity, fire, or sonic damage, such as fireball or lightning bolt. After the spell is cast, lingering tendrils of raw energy persist; creatures damaged by the spell are entangled for 1 round. The spell is considered to be in effect during this time and can be dispelled normally. An entangling spell uses up a spell slot two levels higher than the spell's actual level.

data.name: Envoy Cognizance
data.description: When your elemental envoy is nearby, its associated energy enhances your ability to manifest energy powers.
data.prerequisite: Knowledge (the planes) 1 rank, Psicrystal Affinity, Elemental Envoys
data.benefit: Because you have chosen to replace your psi-crystal with an elemental steward - either an emberling (fire), a geodite (sonic), an arctine (cold), or a tempestan (electricity) - you can now utilize its associated energy to enhance any power you manifest that has the same energy descriptor. While your elemental envoy remains adjacent to you, you manifest these powers at one manifester level higher than normal.

data.name: Epic Ancestral Daisho
data.description: Your ancestral weapon gains the capacity for even greater power.
data.benefit: The maximum weapon bonus that you can awaken in your ancestral daisho increases by +1 (such as from +10 to +11). Also, your ancestral daisho's maximum enhancement bonus increases by +1 (such as from +5 to +6).

data.name: Epic Combat Expertise
data.description: You have extraordinary talent at using your combat skill for defense.
data.prerequisite: Int 19, Epic Prowess, Combat Expertise, base attack bonus +21
data.benefit: When you use the attack action or full attack action in melee, you can take a penalty equal to or less than one-half your base attack bonus (rounded down) on your attack and add the same number to your AC. The modifiers to attack rolls and AC last until your next turn. The bonus to your AC is a dodge bonus.

data.name: Epic Counterspell
data.description: You can counterspell any number of spells each round.
data.prerequisite: Spellcraft 30 ranks, Combat Reflexes, Improved Counterspell, Improved Initiative, Quicken Spell, Reactive Counterspell.
data.benefit: Any number of times per round, you can counterspell an opponent's spell even if you have not readied an action to do so. Such a counterspell doesn't count against your later actions for the round. You can even use this feat when flat-footed.
data.normal: Without this feat, a character must ready an action in each round that she wishes to use a counterspell. A character with the Reactive Counterspell feat can counterspell an opponent's spell if she hasn't readied an action, but only once per turn and not when flat-footed.
data.special: This feat should be considered part of the epic bonus feat list for any class that grants access to spells of at least 6th level as part of normal (non-epic) class progression (such as bards, clerics, druids, sorcerers, and wizards), as well as for any prestige class that grants a "+1 level of existing class" spell progression at all levels.

data.name: Epic Devotion
data.description: Choose an alignment component (chaos, evil, good, or law) different from your own alignment. You are particularly resistant to spells of that alignment.
data.prerequisite: Wis 21, Iron Will, alignment different from the chosen component, patron deity who does not accept clerics who have the chosen component.
data.benefit: You gain a +4 bonus on saves against spells of the chosen alignment component. For instance, a lawful good paladin of Helm who selected "chaos" would gain a +4 bonus on saves against chaotic spells. That paladin could not select "good" or "law" because they are part of her own alignment, nor could she select "evil" because Helm accepts evil clerics.
data.special: You can take this feat more than once. Each time you select the feat, select a new alignment component.

data.name: Epic Divine Might
data.description: You can channel energy to greatly increase the damage you deal in combat.
data.prerequisite: Cha 21, Str 21, Divine Might, Power Attack, ability to turn or rebuke creatures.
data.benefit: Spend one of your turn/rebuke undead attempts to add twice your Charisma bonus to your weapon damage for a number of rounds equal to twice your Charisma modifier. The effects of this feat stack with those granted by Divine Might.

data.name: Epic Divine Resistance
data.description: You can channel energy to temporarily reduce damage you and your allies take from energy sources.
data.prerequisite: Divine Cleansing, Divine Resistance, Extra Turning, ability to turn or rebuke creatures.
data.benefit: Spend one of your turn/rebuke undead attempts to imbue all allies within a 60-foot burst (including yourself) with fire resistance 30, cold resistance 30, and electricity resistance 30. This resistance does not stack with similar resistances, such as those granted by spells or special abilities. This protection lasts for a number of rounds equal to your Charisma modifier (minimum of 1 round).

data.name: Epic Divine Vengeance
data.description: You can channel energy to gain an additional attack against undead in melee.
data.prerequisite: Dex 21, Divine Vengeance, Extra Turning, ability to turn or rebuke creatures.
data.benefit: Spend one of your turn/rebuke undead attempts to gain the ability to make one additional attack each round at your highest base attack bonus with any melee or ranged weapon. This additional attack must be directed at an undead creature. The effects of this feat last for a number of rounds equal to your Charisma modifier (with a minimum of 1 round with the extra attack).

data.name: Epic Divine Vigor
data.description: You impart amazing Constitution and enhanced speed to a number of allies.
data.prerequisite: Cha 19, Divine Vigor, Extra Turning, Great Constitution, ability to turn or rebuke creatures.
data.benefit: Spend one of your turn/rebuke undead attempts to increase your base land speed by 30 feet and gain a +6 sacred bonus to your Constitution. You can impart these bonuses to a number of creatures you are touching, as long as the total number of creatures enhanced does not exceed your Charisma modifier. These enhancements last for a number of minutes equal to your Charisma modifier.

data.name: Epic Dodge
data.description: You are able to evade attacks with exceptional agility.
data.prerequisite: Dex 25, Dodge, Tumble 30 ranks, improved evasion, defensive roll class feature
data.benefit: Once per round, when struck by an attack from an opponent you have designated as the object of your dodge, you may automatically avoid all damage from the attack.

data.name: Epic Endurance
data.description: You are capable of legendary feats of stamina.
data.benefit: Whenever you make a check for performing a physical action that extends over a period of time (running, swimming, holding your breath, and so on), you get a +10 bonus on the check.

data.name: Epic Enervating Touch
data.description: Your touch bestows a powerful surge of negative energy upon creatures.
data.prerequisite: Cha 23, Corrupting Touch, Enervating Touch, non-good alignment.
data.benefit: Your enervating touch bestows two negative levels upon the creature touched. Add +2 to the save DC require to remove the negative levels.

data.name: Epic Evil Brand
data.description: The character is physically marked forever as a mighty servant of evil.
data.benefit: Evil creatures automatically recognize the symbol now emblazoned on the character as a sign of her incredible dark power and reputation for evil. She gains a +6 circumstance bonus on Diplomacy and Intimidate checks made against evil creatures (this stacks with the bonus from the Evil Brand feat).

data.name: Epic Expanded Knowledge
data.description: You learn another power.
data.prerequisite: Character level 21st, ability to manifest powers of the normal maximum power level in at least one psionic class.
data.benefit: You learn one additional power at any level up to the highest level of power you can manifest. You can choose any power, even one that is part of another discipline's list or another class's list.
data.special: You can gain this feat multiple times. Each time, you learn one new power at any level up to the highest level of power you can manifest.

data.name: Epic Fortitude
data.description: You have tremendously high fortitude.
data.benefit: You gain a +4 bonus on all Fortitude saving throws.

data.name: Epic Horrific Appearance
data.description: Your appearance causes tremendous impairment to those who view it.
data.prerequisite: Cha 25, Control Visage, Haunting Appearance, Horrific Appearance, Improved Control Visage, non-good alignment.
data.benefit: Your horrific appearance deals 1d6 points of temporary damage to each viewer's Strength, Dexterity, and Constitution scores.

data.name: Epic Inner Harmony
data.description: You achieve an inner harmony that allows psychic meditation effects to last longer.
data.benefit: The duration for the bonuses gained through psychic meditation is increased to 1 hour per character level.
data.special: Psychic meditation bonuses are always considered temporary bonuses regardless of how long they last and statistic increases do not affect bonus power points or any other ability that relies on a permanent statistic or value. This feat can be taken as a bonus feat by the epic meditant.

data.name: Epic Inspiration
data.description: Your bardic music provides greater inspiration than normally possible.
data.prerequisite: Cha 25, Perform 30 ranks, bardic music class feature
data.benefit: All bonuses granted by your bardic music inspiration abilities are doubled. For example, your inspire courage ability now grants a +4 morale bonus on saving throws against charm and fear effects and a +2 morale bonus on attack and damage rolls; your inspire competence now grants a +4 competence bonus on skill checks; and your inspire greatness ability now grants +4d10 Hit Dice, a +4 competence bonus on attacks, and a +2 competence bonus on Fortitude saves.
data.special: A character can gain this feat multiple times. Its effects stack. Remember that two doublings equals a tripling, and so forth.

data.name: Epic Ki Frenzy
data.description: Your ki frenzy grants you even greater power and agility.
data.benefit: Your ki frenzy grants you a +4 bonus to Strength and Dexterity and increases your speed by 20 feet.
data.normal: Without this feat, ki frenzy grants only a +2 bonus to Strength and Dexterity and a speed increase of 10 feet.

data.name: Epic Leadership
data.description: You attract more powerful cohorts and followers than normally possible.
data.benefit: You attract a cohort and followers as shown on Table 1-33: Epic Leadership.
data.normal: Without this feat, you must use Table 2-25. leadership in the DUNGEON MASTER'S Guide to determine your cohort and followers.

data.name: Epic Manifestation
data.description: Can manifest %1 epic powers per day.|EpicPowerManifest See Epic Spellcasting
data.benefit: Can manifest %1 epic powers per day.|EpicPowerManifest See Epic Spellcasting

data.name: Epic Of The Lost King
data.description: You can channel the power of your bardic music to reinvigorate your allies.
data.benefit: As a move action, you can expend one daily use of your bardic music ability to remove fatigue from up to three allies (including yourself) within 30 feet. If you spend three daily uses of bardic music, you can remove exhaustion from your allies instead.

data.name: Epic Poison Crafter
data.description: The character knows secrets of poison crafting best left forgotten by mortals.
data.benefit: The character can craft the epic poisons described in Epic Poisons.

data.name: Epic Power Penetration
data.description: See Epic Spell Penetration Greater Power Penetration
data.benefit: See Epic Spell Penetration Greater Power Penetration

data.name: Epic Prowess
data.description: You gain great skill in combat.
data.benefit: Gain a +1 bonus on all attacks.
data.special: A character can gain this feat multiple times. Its effects stack.

data.name: Epic Psionic Endowment
data.description: See Epic Spell Focus Greater Psionic Endowment
data.benefit: See Epic Spell Focus Greater Psionic Endowment

data.name: Epic Psionic Focus
data.description: You can expend your psionic focus to greater effect.
data.prerequisite: Character level 21st, ability to manifest powers of the normal maximum power level in at least one psionic class.
data.benefit: When you expend your psionic focus, you can simultaneously use two feats that require a psionic focus to be expended, instead of just one. The feats must be stackable (for instance, Burrowing Power and Extend Power could both be used together on the same power). You must still spend the requisite power points required to use each metapsionic feat, and you cannot exceed the power point limit set by your manifester level.
data.special: You can take this feat multiple times. Each time you do so, you can simultaneously use one additional feat that requires a psionic focus to be expended.

data.name: Epic Psychic Meditation
data.description: You further your study and understanding of psychic energy centers, which allows you to enter the deepest levels of meditation.
data.prerequisite: 21+ levels, Intense Psychic Meditation (seven times), Psychic Meditation (seven times)
data.benefit: When you take this feat, you are granted additional psychic energy center bonuses. You can select a given psychic energy center only once. You can take this feat multiple times and must select a psychic energy center that you have not already previously selected. Bonuses from this feat stack with bonuses received from the Psychic Meditation and Intense Psychic Meditation feats. Meditation time is increased to 40 minutes. The duration of the benefits increase to 4 hours. You can activate this feat only once per day. This feat can be taken as a bonus feat by the epic meditant.

data.name: Epic Reflexes
data.description: You have tremendously fast reflexes.
data.benefit: You gain a +4 bonus on all Reflex saving throws.

data.name: Epic Reputation
data.description: Your reputation provides great bonuses on interactions with others.
data.benefit: You gain a +4 bonus on Bluff, Diplomacy, Gather Information, Intimidate, and Perform checks.

data.name: Epic Skill Focus
data.description: Choose a skill, such as Move Silently. You have a legendary knack with that skill.
data.benefit: You gain a +10 bonus on all skill checks with that skill.
data.special: You can gain this feat multiple times. Its effects do not stack. Each time you take the feat, it applies to a different skill.

data.name: Epic Speed
data.description: You can move much more quickly than a normal person
data.benefit: Your speed increases by 30 feet. This benefit does not stack with increased speed granted by magic items or non-permanent magical effects.
data.special: This feat only functions when you are wearing medium armor, light armor, or no armor.

data.name: Epic Spell Focus
data.description: Choose a school of magic, such as Illusion. Your spells of that school are far more potent than normal.
data.prerequisite: Greater Spell Focus and Spell Focus in the school selected, ability to cast at least one 9th-level spell of the school to be chosen
data.benefit: Add +6 to the Difficulty Class for all saving throws against spells from the school of magic you select to focus on. This overlaps (does not stack with) the bonuses from Spell Focus and Greater Spell Focus.
data.special: You can gain this feat multiple times. Its effects do not stack. Each time you take the feat, it applies to a different school of magic.

data.name: Epic Spell Penetration
data.description: Your spells are tremendously potent, breaking through spell resistance with ease.
data.benefit: You get a +6 bonus on caster level checks to beat a creature's spell resistance. This overlaps (does not stack with) the bonuses from Spell Penetration and Greater Spell Penetration.

data.name: Epic Spell Secret
data.description: Choose a single spell that you know. You can apply one of your metamagic feats to that spell without altering the spell's level.
data.prerequisite: Wu jen level 21, Spellcraft 27 ranks, three or more metamagic feats
data.benefit: You choose a spell that you know, which becomes permanently modified as though affected by any one of your metamagic feats. The spell's level does not change, and once the choice of spell and modification are chosen, they cannot be changed. You can apply this feat's effect to a spell already modified by a spell secret, but you can't apply Epic Spell Secret to the same spell more than once.

data.name: Epic Spell Specialization
data.description: Choose a spell that you can cast. That spell is more powerful when you cast it.
data.prerequisite: Spellcraft 24 ranks, ability to cast 9th-level spells
data.benefit: When casting the chosen spell, treat your caster level as 2 higher for all purposes, including range, duration, resistance to dispel checks, and overcoming spell resistance.

data.name: Epic Spellcasting
data.description: You can create and cast spells that transcend the most powerful existing spells.
data.prerequisite: Spellcraft 24 ranks, Knowledge (arcana) 24 ranks, ability to cast 9th-level arcane spells OR Spellcraft 24 ranks, Knowledge (religion) 24 ranks, ability to cast 9th-level divine spells OR Spellcraft 24 ranks, Knowledge (nature) 24 ranks, ability to cast 9th-level divine spell
data.benefit: You may develop and cast epic spells, as detailed in Chapter 2 of the Epic Level Handbook. If you are an arcane spellcaster, you may cast a number of epic spells per day equal to your ranks in Knowledge (arcana) divided by 10. If you are a divine spellcaster, you may cast a number of epic spells per day equal to your ranks in Knowledge (religion) or Knowledge(nature) divided by 10.
data.special: If you meet more than one set of prerequisites, the limit on the number of spells you may cast per day is cumulative. For example, if you are a wizard/cleric, you may cast a number of arcane epic spells per day equal to your number of ranks in Knowledge (arcana), divided by ten, and a number of divine epic spells per day equal to your number of ranks in Knowledge (religion), divided by ten.

data.name: Epic Spellfire Wielder
data.description: Your can store more spellfire energy levels than normal.
data.benefit: For the purpose of determining your limit of stored spellfire energy levels, treat your Constitution score as if it were 4 points higher than it is.
data.normal: Without this feat, a spellfire wielder's limit of stored spellfire energy levels is equal to her constitution score.
data.special: You can select Epic Spellfire Wielder multiple times. Each time you take this feat, your limit of stored spellfire energy levels increases by 4.

data.name: Epic Sunder
data.description: You deal extra damage when attacking objects
data.prerequisite: Str 25, Epic Prowess, Improved Sunder, Power Attack
data.benefit: When attacking objects, you may double any extra damage derived from Strength. When attempting to break an object with sudden force rather than dealing damage, you gain a +4 bonus on your Strength check.

data.name: Epic Toughness
data.description: You are preternaturally tough.
data.benefit: You gain +20 hit points.
data.special: A character can gain this feat multiple times. Its effects stack.

data.name: Epic Trigger Power
data.description: You choose one power that you can manifest for free.
data.prerequisite: Inner Strength, Talented, Trigger Power, reserve power points (see below)
data.benefit: Choose any 4th-, 5th-, or 6th-level power you know. From now on you can attempt to trigger that power without paying its cost. To trigger a power, you must first satisfy the requirement of a power point reserve equal to or greater than what it would normally cost you to manifest the power. Then, make an ability check appropriate for the power's discipline (for instance, you would make a Charisma check for charm person). If you meet or exceed the ability check DC (see below), the power is manifested normally with no power point cost. If you fail the check, you are forced to pay its cost, but the power is still manifested normally. You can't use Epic Trigger Power in conjunction with psionic attack or defense modes.DCs for the ability check are determined by level: 4th level, 19; 5th level, 21; and 6th level, 23.
data.special: You can take this feat multiple times, each time using it for a new triggered power. This feat can be taken as a bonus feat by the epic metamind.

data.name: Epic Weapon Focus
data.description: Choose one type of weapon, such as greataxe. You are especially good at using this weapon.
data.prerequisite: Weapon Focus in the weapon to be chosen
data.benefit: Add a +2 bonus to all attack rolls you make using the selected weapon.
data.special: You can gain this feat multiple times. Its effects do not stack. Each time you take the feat, it applies to a different type of weapon.

data.name: Epic Weapon Specialization
data.description: Choose one type of weapon, such as greataxe. You deal extraordinary damage wielding this weapon.
data.prerequisite: Epic Weapon Focus, Weapon Focus, Weapon Specialization (all in the weapon to be chosen)
data.benefit: Add +4 to all damage you deal using the selected weapon. If the weapon is a ranged weapon, the damage bonus only applies if the target is within 30 feet.
data.special: You can gain this feat multiple times. Its effects do not stack. Each time you take the feat, it applies to a different type of weapon.

data.name: Epic Will
data.description: You have tremendously strong willpower.
data.benefit: You gain a +4 bonus on all Will saving throws.

data.name: Epic Witchlight
data.description: Your witchlight grows in duration, strength, and power of illumination.
data.benefit: Your witchlight effects grow in power, as follows:

data.name: Eschew Materials
data.description: You can cast spells without relying on material components.
data.benefit: You can cast any spell that has a material component costing 1 gp or less without needing that component. (The casting of the spell still provokes attacks of opportunity as normal.) If the spell requires a material component that costs more than 1 gp, you must have the material component at hand to cast the spell. just as normal.

data.name: Eternal Strength
data.description: You have taken Kord's fighting ways to heart. Throwing yourself into every brawl, you draw upon your mighty deity's strength.
data.benefit: You gain a +4 bonus on saving throws to resist effects that deal Strength damage or cause Strength drain.In addition, you may add the following spells to your cleric spell list:

data.name: Ethereal Leap
data.description: Your teleports can cover a greater distance.
data.benefit: The distance you can transfer via your Ethereal Sidestep feat increases by 30 feet.
data.special: You may select this feat multiple times. Each time you choose the feat, the distance covered by your Ethereal Sidestep increases by 30 feet.

data.name: Ethereal Sidestep
data.description: You can teleport yourself a short distance.
data.benefit: As a standard action, you can transfer yourself up to 30 feet from your current position, as the spell dimension door.

data.name: Ethereal Tendril
data.description: After a daylong ceremony in which you sacrifice and devour an ether scarab (described in Monster Manual II), you can imbue your spells with the power to strike incorporeal foes.
data.benefit: You may apply the ghost touch template to your spells. You also gain the ability to move at normal speed (instead of half speed) when traveling on the Ethereal Plane.

data.name: Ethran
data.description: You have been initiated into the secrets of the Witches of Rashemen as a member of the Ethran (the "untrained").
data.benefit: You gain a +2 bonus on Handle Animal and Survival checks. When dealing with other Rashemis, you gain a +2 bonus on Charisma-based skill and ability checks. Furthermore, you can participate in circle magic (see Circle Magic).
data.special: You may select this feat only as a 1st-level character. You may have only one regional feat.

data.name: Ettercap Berserker
data.description: The intense physical training required to join your lodge has made you tougher.
data.prerequisite: Ability to rage, membership in the Ettercap berserker lodge (see Berserker Lodges). Region: Rashemen
data.benefit: You gain a +2 bonus on saving throws against poison. When raging, you gain a +6 bonus to Constitution. During a greater rage, you instead gain a +8 bonus to constitution.
data.normal: A character in a rage gains a +4 bonus to Constitution. A character in a greater rage gains a +6 bonus to Constitution.

data.name: Euphoric Reduction
data.description: Channel your euphoric surge into a boost for one of your skills.
data.benefit: When you trigger your surging euphoria, you can choose to channel it into a concentrated boost of physical resilience. Instead of gaining a bonus on attack rolls, damage rolls, and saving throws, you gain damage reduction equal to three times the surging euphoria level to which you have access.For instance, a 6th-level wilder with this feat who uses wild surge to boost her manifester level by two and uses surging euphoria can gain damage reduction 3/- for 2 rounds.

data.name: Evasive Reflexes
data.description: When an opponent gives you an opening in combat, you know exactly what to do: slip away.
data.benefit: When an opponent gives you a chance to make an attack of opportunity, you can instead immediately take 5-foot step.
data.special: Evasive Reflexes can be used in place of Combat Reflexes to qualify for a feat, prestige class, or other special ability. You can take both this feat and Combat Reflexes.

data.name: Evil Brand
data.description: The character is physically marked forever as a servant of an evil power greater than herself or as a villain who does not care who knows that she seeks only death, destruction, and misery for others. The symbol is unquestionable in its perversity, depicting a depravity so unthinkable that all who see it know beyond a doubt that the bearer is forever in the sway of the blackest powers.
data.benefit: Evil creatures automatically recognize the symbol now emblazoned upon the character as a sign of her utter depravity or discipleship to a powerful patron, although the specific identity of the patron is not revealed. She gains a +2 circumstance bonus on Diplomacy and Intimidate checks made against evil creatures.

data.name: Evil Devotion
data.description: The power of evil cloaks you and your allies.
data.benefit: Once per day as an immediate action, you can surround yourself with an aura of evil that grants you and each of your allies within 30 feet damage reduction that can be overcome only by good-aligned weapons. The numeric value of this damage reduction is 1 + 1/five character levels you possess (maximum 5/good at 20th level), in addition, your and your allies' natural and weapon attacks are evil-aligned for the purpose of overcoming damage reduction. This effect lasts for 1 minute.
data.special: You can select this feat multiple times, gaining one additional daily use each time you take it.If you have the ability to turn or rebuke undead, you gain one additional daily use of this feat for each daily turn or rebuke use you expend.

data.name: Evil Embraced
data.description: You embrace the power of your fiendish patron and call upon that power in moments of great need.
data.benefit: Once per day, as an immediate action, you can call upon your fiendish patron to steel you against harm. You gain damage reduction 10/good for 1 round (until the start of your next turn).

data.name: Eviscerator
data.description: The allies of your foes are especially afraid of your critical hits.
data.prerequisite: Cha 13, undead type, Death Master, Daunting Presence, Improved Critical, base attack bonus +1.
data.benefit: Whenever you score a critical hit with a melee attack against a living foe, creatures within 30 feet that are allied to that foe are shaken for 1 minute. This is a mind-affecting, feat effect.

data.name: Evocation Resistance
data.description: You suffer less damage from evocation spells.
data.benefit: You take 1 less point of damage per die rolled from evocation spells. For example, if struck by a fireball cast by a 5th-level caster, you take 5d6-5 points of damage.

data.name: Evolved
data.description: Your mutation works only to your advantage
data.benefit: Choose a mutation template you possess. You can ignore one ore more drawbacks of your chosen mutation template. See each mutation template's description for effects of this feat.
data.special: You may gain this feat multiple times. Its effects do not stack. Each time you take this feat, it affects a different mutation template.

data.name: Exalted Companion
data.description: Instead of an animal companion, you have a magical beast of good alignment.
data.prerequisite: Able to acquire a new animal companion, minimum level requirement (see below).
data.benefit: When choosing an animal companion, you may choose a magical beast as shown on the table below. You must choose a companion whose alignment is the same as yours, so only a ranger can have a blink dog, pegasus, or unicorn as a companion. Even though your companion is a magical beast, yon can cast spells on it as though it were an animal. The exalted companion has all the normal abilities of a typical creature of its kind, as well as the characteristics of an animal companion determined by the druid or ranger's level.

data.name: Exalted Smite
data.description: Your smite ability is empowered with holy energy.
data.benefit: When you use your smite evil ability, your weapon is considered good-aligned for purposes of bypassing damage reduction.

data.name: Exalted Spell Resistance
data.description: You are particularly resistant to evil spells.
data.benefit: Against evil spells (spells with the evil designator) and spell-like abilities used by evil outsiders, your spell resistance increases by +4.

data.name: Exalted Turning
data.description: You turn undead with such power that affected undead take damage.
data.benefit: Any undead creature that you turn takes 3d6 points of damage in addition to the normal turning effect.

data.name: Exalted Wild Shape
data.description: You can use your wild shape ability to take the form of a good aligned magical beast.
data.prerequisite: Wild shape class ability, wild shaping class level 8th.
data.benefit: You can use your wild shape ability to turn yourself into a blink dog, giant eagle, giant owl, pegasus, or unicorn. You can also turn into a celestial version of any animal you could normally transform into. (At the DM's discretion, other magical beast forms might be available to you. In general, only creatures of CR 3 or lower are possible.) You gain the extraordinary and supernatural abilities of the creature. This ability counts as a use of your wild shape ability and functions the same way

data.name: Exceptional Artisan
data.description: You are an expert at creating magic items faster than usual.
data.benefit: When determining the time you need to craft any item, reduce the base time by 25%.

data.name: Exceptional Deflection
data.description: You can deflect any type of ranged attack.
data.prerequisite: Dex 21, Wis 19, Deflect Arrows, Improved Unarmed Strike
data.benefit: You can deflect any ranged attacks (including spells that require ranged touch attacks) as if they were arrows. If deflecting a spell, add the spell level to the DC to deflect the attack.

data.name: Excised From The Web Of Life
data.description: A being spun out of madness has granted you the power to make the children of nature quail, but now you must endure their loathing.
data.prerequisite: Ability to prepare and cast blight, required sacrifice.
data.benefit: Whenever you successfully cast a spell upon any creature of the animal, fey, or plant type you may choose to deal damage equal to your caster level to the target, even if the spell does not normally deal damage. If the spell cast allows a saving throw and the target succeeds it takes no damage from this ability. If the spell cast does not allow a saving throw the target can make a Fort save to negate this damage.
data.special: A wizard may select Excised From the Web of Life as a wizard bonus feat.Sacrifice: You suffer a penalty equal to your level on all Charisma-based skills and checks when dealing with creatures of the animal, fey, and plant types as well as anyone with the wild empathy class ability.

data.name: Exercises of Arnd
data.description: You follow the teachings of the legendary ancient Oeridian general-priest Arnd of Tdon, practicing a rigidly defined set of physical exercises every day to maintain peak fighting effectiveness.
data.prerequisite: Toughness. Region: Furyondy, Nyrond, Oeridian, Ratik (Oerth).
data.benefit: You practice a daily regimen of exercises that grant you special bonuses in combat. You choose which bonus you'd prefer before conducting your exercise, modifying your regimen slightly to achieve the desired effect. Granted effects last until the next time you perform the exercises of Arnd.Conducting exercises of Arnd require 8 hours of rest to clear the mind. You do not have to slumber for every minute of this time, but you must refrain from movement, combat, spellcasting, skill use, conversation, or any other fairly demanding physical or mental task during the rest period. If your rest is interrupted, each interruption adds hour to the total amount of time you must rest in order to clear your mind, and you must have at least 1 hour of uninterrupted rest immediately prior to conducting the exercises of Arnd. If you do not need to sleep for some reason, you still must have 8 hours of restful calm before conducting the exercises.Rite of Battle: You get a +1 morale bonus on melee attack rolls with a specific weapon you incorporate into your exercise.Rite of Calm: You get a +1 resistance bonus on all saving throws.Rite of Fitness: You gain +6 temporary hit points.

data.name: Exhaled Barrier
data.description: You can use your breath weapon to create a wall of energy.
data.benefit: When you use your breath weapon, you can choose to create a 10-foot-by-10-foot vertical plane of energy instead of producing its normal effect. The opaque wall's near endpoint begins at any corner of your space and extends in a straight line for 10 feet or until it contacts a solid surface. The wall lasts for 1d4 rounds. Any creature passing through the wall takes damage equal to that normally dealt by your breath weapon. If you create the wall so that it appears where creatures are, each creature takes damage as if passing through the wall. Either way, a successful Reflex save (DC 10 + 1/2 your HD + your Con modifier) halves this damage.If your breath weapon doesn't deal energy damage, the wall deals fire damage. if your breath weapon doesn't deal damage, the wall deals 2d6 points of fire damage.

data.name: Exhaled Immunity
data.description: You can use your breath weapon to grant a willing creature immunity to energy.
data.benefit: When you use your breath weapon, instead of producing its normal effect, you can choose to grant one willing creature adjacent to you immunity to the energy type of your breath weapon. If your breath weapon doesn't deal energy damage, this feat grants immunity to fire. This immunity lasts for 1d4 rounds. You can't use this feat on yourself.

data.name: Exhausting Defense
data.description: Your fighting style invites attackers to wear themselves out against your stalwart defense.
data.prerequisite: Combat Expertise, Dodge, base attack bonus +5.
data.benefit: The Exhausting Defense feat enables the use of three tactical maneuvers.Fatigue the Foe: To use this maneuver, you must fight using the Combat Expertise feat for at least three consecutive rounds against the same opponent, whom you have also designated as the target of your Dodge feat. The penalty you take on your attacks must be -2 or more. After the first three rounds, each round your opponent continues to attack you he must make a DC 15 Fortitude save or become fatigued. If you stop attacking that opponent, or he ceases attacking you, you must restart the three rounds fighting with Combat Expertise before using this maneuver against that opponent again. An opponent affected by this maneuver remains fatigued until the end of the encounter.Exhaust the Foe: To use this maneuver, you must first face a fatigued opponent. In addition, you must fight using the Combat Expertise feat for at least three consecutive rounds against the same opponent, whom you have also designated as the target of your Dodge feat. Rounds that count toward your Fatigue the Foe maneuver do not count for this maneuver. The penalty you take on your attacks must be -4 or more. Beginning on the third round your target attacks you, it must make a DC 15 Fortitude save or become exhausted. If you break off combat with your target, you must restart the two rounds fighting with Combat Expertise before using this maneuver against that creature again. If you stop attacking that opponent, or he ceases attacking you, you must restart the two rounds fighting with Combat Expertise before using this maneuver against that opponent again. An opponent affected by this maneuver remains exhausted until the end of the encounter, at which time he becomes fatigued until he rests.The Best Offense: To use this maneuver, you must face a fatigued or exhausted opponent. You must then spend a round using the total defense action. The round after that, you must then attack your foe without using Combat Expertise at all. For that round, your foe is flat-footed against your attacks and you gain a +2 bonus on all attacks against that foe until your next turn.
data.special: Constructs, oozes, undead, and creatures immune to fatigue are immune to these maneuvers.A fighter may select Exhausting Defense as one of his fighter bonus feats.

data.name: Exotic Armor Proficiency
data.description: Choose a type of exotic armor, such as mammoth leather or tumbler's breastplate. You understand how to wear that type of exotic armor properly.
data.prerequisite: Armor Proficiency of the appropriate sort (for example, you must have Armor Proficiency [light] to take Exotic Armor Proficiency (mammoth leather)).
data.benefit: You are proficient with a specific type of exotic armor and take no armor non-proficiency penalties when you wear it).
data.normal: A character who is wearing armor with which she is not proficient applies its armor check penalty on attack rolls and on all Strength- and Dexterity-based ability checks and skill checks.
data.special: You can gain this feat multiple times. Each time you take the feat, it applies to a new type of exotic armor.

data.name: Exotic Shield Proficiency
data.description: Choose an exotic shield, such as a gauntlet shield or battle cloak. You are proficient with that type of exotic shield.
data.benefit: You are proficient with a specific type of exotic shield and take no shield non-proficiency penalties when you wear it).
data.normal: A character who is using a shield with which she is not proficient applies its armor check penalty on attack rolls and on all Strength- and Dexterity-based ability checks and skill checks.
data.special: As a variant rule, the DM can allow fighters to choose one free Exotic Shield Proficiency feat instead of the Tower Shield Proficiency feat they receive at 1st level.A fighter may select Exotic Shield Proficiency as one of her fighter bonus feats.

data.name: Exotic Weapon Proficiency
data.description: Choose any type of exotic weapon, such as dire flail or shuriken (see Weapons for a list of exotic weapons). You understand how to use that type of exotic weapon in combat.
data.prerequisite: Base attack bonus +1 (plus Str 13 for bastard sword or dwarven waraxe)
data.benefit: You make attack rolls with the weapon normally.
data.normal: A character who uses a weapon with which he or she is not proficient takes a -4 penalty on attack rolls.
data.special: You can gain this feat multiple times. Each time you take the feat, it applies to a new weapon. Proficiency with the bastard sword or the dwarven waraxe has a prerequisite of Str 13.

data.name: Expanded Aura Of Courage
data.description: Your aura of courage protects more allies than normal.
data.benefit: Without this feat, your aura of courage only works on allies within 10 feet.

data.name: Expanded Ki Pool
data.description: You know the secret mantras that grant you greater understanding of your ki.
data.benefit: You gain three extra daily uses of your ki power (ki power is a class feature of the ninja).

data.name: Expanded Knowledge
data.description: You learn another power.
data.benefit: Add to your powers known one additional power of any level up to one level lower than the highest-level power you can manifest. For example, a 7th-level psion gains either a new 1st, 2nd, or 3rd-level power. You can choose any power, including powers from another discipline's list or even from another class's list.
data.special: You can gain this feat multiple times. Each time, you learn one new power at any level up to one less than the highest-level power you can manifest.

data.name: Expanded Possession
data.description: You can ride or possess an additional type of creature.
data.benefit: Choose one type of creature, such as giants. You may use any feat from the path of the dominator on that sort of creature as if it were a creature of your own type. For example, a human who selected this feat and chose giants would be able to use Ghost Ride on humanoids or giants. Choosing a creature type that is not affected by mind-affecting abilities (such as constructs, oozes, and undead) has no effect.
data.special: You can gain this feat multiple times. Each time, you choose a new type of creature to be affected.

data.name: Expanded Soulmeld Capacity
data.description: Your soul's tie to incarnum allows you to maintain more essentia in a single soulmeld.
data.benefit: When you shape your soulmelds, choose one soulmeld. Your essentia capacity for that soulmeld is increased by 1 (up to a maximum of your Constitution bonus).Each time you shape your soulmelds, you can change the soulmeld that benefits from this feat.
data.special: You can take this feat multiple times. For each time you take this feat, you can apply its effects to one additional soulmeld while shaping. You can't apply this feat's effects more than once to the same soulmeld.

data.name: Expeditious Dodge
data.description: You're good at avoiding attacks while moving quickly.
data.benefit: When you move 40 feet or more in a single turn, you gain a +2 dodge bonus to your Armor Class until the beginning of your next turn.
data.special: Expeditious Dodge can be used in place of the Dodge feat to qualify for a feat, prestige class, or other special ability. A fighter may select Expeditious Dodge as one of his fighter bonus feats.

data.name: Expel Vestige
data.description: You can expel a vestige to which you are bound before the duration of its pact with you has expired.
data.benefit: Once per day, you can attempt to expel a vestige to which you are bound. To do so, you must draw its seal and go through the entire process of summoning it again. If you succeed on the new binding check, you expel the vestige before it would normally leave you, and you can summon a different one to replace it if you wish. Regardless of your success or failure in expelling the vestige, you take a -10 penalty on your next binding check with any vestige, and apply the same penalty on your binding check the next time you summon the vestige you expelled.
data.normal: A bound vestige does not leave you until 24 hours have passed since its summoning.

data.name: Expert Dungeoneer
data.description: Your experience and study of dungeons have given you insight on common trapsetting techniques. Region: Blackmoor, deep gnome, Greyhawk, hill dwarf, mountain dwarf.
data.benefit: You receive a +4 competence bonus on saving throws made against the effects of mechanical traps.

data.name: Expert Siege Engineer
data.description: You are particularly skilled at operating siege weapons, such as catapults and battering rams.
data.benefit: You gain a +2 competence bonus on attack rolls and damage rolls made when using a siege engine.

data.name: Expert Swimmer
data.description: You swim like a fish. You can stay underwater far longer than others of your race, and you are at home in the water.
data.benefit: You can hold your breath for 3 rounds per point of Constitution. You gain a +4 bonus on Constitution checks made to continue holding your breath. On a successful Swim check, you swim your land speed (as a full-round action) or half your land speed (as a move action).Your natural swim speed increases by 10 feet, if you have a swim speed.
data.normal: You can hold your breath for a number of rounds equal to twice your Constitution before you are at risk of drowning. On a successful Swim check, you swim half your land speed as a full-round action, or one-quarter your land speed as a move action.

data.name: Expert Tactician
data.description: Your tactical skills work to your advantage.
data.prerequisite: Dex 13, Combat Reflexes, base attack bonus +2.
data.benefit: If you hit a creature with an attack of opportunity, you and all your allies gain a +2 circumstance bonus on melee attack rolls and damage rolls against that creature for 1 round.

data.name: Expert Tumbler
data.description: You can tumble with exceptional deftness.
data.benefit: You may use the Tumble skill at normal speed with only a -5 penalty.
data.normal: Using the Tumble skill at full speed incurs a -10 check penalty.

data.name: Exploit Adjustment
data.description: Your skill at finding and exploiting opportunities is such that you can find openings in even the smallest movements.
data.benefit: You may make an attack of opportunity against any opponent who moves from one square adjacent to you to another square adjacent to you.
data.normal: Taking a 1-foot step does not provoke attacks of opportunity.
data.special: A fighter may select Exploit Adjustment as one of his fighter bonus feats.

data.name: Explosive Spell
data.description: You can cast spells that blast creatures off their feet.
data.benefit: On a failed Reflex save, an explosive spell ejects any creature caught its area, sending it to a location outside the nearest edge of that area, dealing additional damage and further knocking creatures prone. For example, all creatures in the area of an explosive fireball that fail their saving throws not only take full damage but are pushed to the closest square outside the perimeter of the spell's 20-foot-radius spread. likewise, an explosive lightning bolt moves targets that fail their saves to outside the area defined by the squares the bolt's line passes through. Any creature moved in this manner also takes an additional 1d6 points of damage per 10 feet moved (no additional damage if moved less than 10 feet by the effect) and is knocked prone. If some obstacle prevents a blasted creature from being moved to the edge of the effect, the creature is stopped and takes 1d6 points of damage from striking the barrier (in addition to any damage taken from the distance moved before then). In any event, this movement does not provoke attacks of opportunity. Explosive Spell can be applied only to spells that allow Reflex saves and affect an area (a cone, cylinder, line, or burst). An explosive spell uses up a spell slot two levels higher than the spell's actual level.

data.name: Extend Mystery
data.description: You can cast mysteries that last longer than normal.
data.benefit: Once per day, you can apply the effect of the Extend Mystery feat to any mystery you cast. An extended mystery lasts twice as long as normal.Casting an extended mystery is a full-round action.
data.special: You can take this feat multiple times.

data.name: Extend Power
data.description: You can manifest powers that last longer than normal.
data.benefit: To use this feat, you must expend your psionic focus (see Concentration skill description). You can manifest an extended power. An extended power lasts twice as long as normal. A power with a duration of concentration, instantaneous, or permanent is not affected by this feat.Using this feat increases the power point cost of the power by 2. The power's total cost cannot exceed your manifester level.

data.name: Extend Rage
data.description: You are able to maintain your rage longer than most.
data.benefit: Each of the uses of your rage or frenzy ability lasts an additional 5 rounds beyond its normal duration.
data.special: You can take this feat multiple times. Its effects stack.

data.name: Extend Spell
data.description: You can cast spells that last longer than normal.
data.benefit: An extended spell lasts twice as long as normal. A spells with a concentration, instantaneous, or permanent duration is not affected by this feat. An extended spell uses up a spell slot one level higher than the spell's actual level.

data.name: Extend Spreading Breath
data.description: You can convert your breath weapon into a spread effect that can be used at range.
data.prerequisite: Con 15, breath weapon, Shape Breath, Spreading Breath, size Small or larger.
data.benefit: You can modify your breath weapon so that it fills a spread centered anywhere within a short distance of your head. The range and size of the spread depends on your size, as shown below.

data.name: Extend Supernatural Ability
data.description: You can cause a supernatural ability with a duration to last longer than normal.
data.benefit: Once per day, you can extend a supernatural ability that has a duration. When you use an extended supernatural ability, it lasts twice as long as normal. A supernatural ability that has a duration of concentration, that happens instantaneously, or that permanently affects a target is not affected by this feat. You must declare the use of this feat before you use the ability. Extending a supernatural ability does not require a separate action.
data.special: This feat can be taken multiple times. Each time it is taken, you can extend a supernatural ability one additional time per day. You cannot extend a a particular use of a supernatural ability more than once.

data.name: Extend Utterance
data.description: Your utterances have a more lasting effect on the universe.
data.prerequisite: Truespeak 6 ranks, ability to speak utterances
data.benefit: An extended utterance lasts twice as long as normal. An utterance with a duration of concentration or instantaneous is not affected by this feat. An extended utterance has a Truespeak DC 5 higher than normal.

data.name: Extended Construction
data.description: You gain an increased duration on your manifested astral constructs.
data.prerequisite: Manifester level 3rd+, Augment Construction, Ecto Manipulation
data.benefit: Astral constructs that you manifest have a duration of 1 minute per manifester level instead of their normal duration. For example a 13th-level psion manifests astral construct VI, creating a single type VI construct with a duration of 13 minutes

data.name: Extended Full Manifestation
data.description: You can manifest fully for an extended period of time.
data.benefit: When incorporeal, as a standard action you can make a Will saving throw (DC 15) to manifest fully for 1 minute.
data.normal: A ghost without this feat cannot manifest fully outside the Manifest Ward without magical aid, or (if he has the Full Manifestation feat) can manifest only for a single round.

data.name: Extended Life Span
data.description: You are exceptionally long-lived.
data.benefit: Add one-half the maximum result of your race's maximum age modifier to your normal middle age, old, and venerable age categories. For example, a human who took this feat would reach middle age at 58 years (rather than 38), old age at 73 years (instead of 53), and venerable age at 90 years (instead of 70). Calculate the character's maximum age using the new venerable number. This feat can't lower your current age category (for instance, if you're already middle age but the feat pushes the middle age category to above your current age, you don't revert to adulthood).
data.special: You can gain this feat multiple times. Its effects stack.

data.name: Extended Rage
data.description: Your rage lasts longer than it normally would.
data.benefit: Each of your rages lasts an additional 3 rounds beyond its normal duration.
data.special: You can take this feat multiple times, and the additional rounds stack.

data.name: Extended Reach
data.description: Your flexible body allows you to reach farther than normal.
data.prerequisite: Small or larger size, nonrigid body or a nonrigid attack form such as a tentacle, feeler, or pseudopod.
data.benefit: Your body or a part of your body with which you can deliver a melee attack is boneless and flexible, allowing you to threaten a larger than normal area with melee attacks. Add +5 feet to your normal reach.

data.name: Extended Spirit Form
data.description: You can channel your chastise spirit ability to increase the duration of your spirit form.
data.prerequisite: Chastise spirits class ability, spirit form class ability.
data.benefit: You can spend a use of your chastise spirit ability to remain in spirit form for a number of additional minutes equal to 1 + your Charisma modifier (minimum 1 additional minute).
data.normal: A spirit shaman's spirit form allows her to become incorporeal for 1 minute.

data.name: Extra Arms
data.description: Your construct has more than the standard number of arms.
data.prerequisite: Construct: Construct, must have a slam attack.Creator: Craft Construct, CL 14th.
data.benefit: A construct with this augmentation gains two more arms than normal and may make two additional slam attacks during a full attack action. These additional slam attacks are treated in all ways as the construct's usual slam attacks, with the same attack and damage modifiers.A stone golem with extra arms costs 112,500 gp and requires a spellcaster to spend 112 days, 62,500 gp, and 4,500 XP to create.
data.special: Cost: +25% of the construct's market price and cost.CR Adjustment: +1

data.name: Extra Aura
data.description: You gain the aura ability of a mantle you have donned.
data.prerequisite: Don Mantle, psychic aura class feature.
data.benefit: You gain the ability to generate a psychic aura based on a mantle you have accessed with the Don Mantle feat. When you generate a psychic aura, you can also choose the aura granted by the mantle from the associated Don Mantle feat.
data.special: You can gain this feat multiple times. Each time you do, you choose a new mantle you have donned with the Don Mantle feat.

data.name: Extra Contacts
data.description: You make connections and alliances easily.
data.benefit: Your maximum number of contacts increases by four.
data.normal: Without this feat, a character is normally limited to a number of contacts equal to his Charisma modifier (minimum 1).
data.special: You can take the Extra Contacts feat multiple times. Its effects stack. Each time you take the feat, you add another four to your maximum number of contacts.

data.name: Extra Divine Power
data.description: You can use one of your divine granted abilities more often.
data.prerequisite: A divinely granted class ability with a set number of uses per day.
data.benefit: Choose a class ability you have from a class that grants divine spells and has a set number of uses per day, such as a shugenja's sense elements or a spirit shaman's spirit form. You may not select an ability granted by a domain. You may use the chosen ability two more times per day.
data.special: You may gain this feat more than once. Its effects do not stack. Each time you take the feat, it applies to a different class ability.

data.name: Extra Domain Spell
data.description: You have chosen to be more specialized in a particular domain.
data.prerequisite: Wis 15, access to one domain spell.
data.benefit: Choose one domain spell that you can cast. You may cast this spell one extra time each day Once this spell is chosen, it may not be changed.
data.special: You may take this feat multiple times. Each time you choose this feat, you select a different domain spell to which it applies.

data.name: Extra Edge
data.description: Your ability to deal spell damage is particularly striking.
data.benefit: You gain a +1 bonus on your warmage edge, plus an additional +1 bonus per four warmage levels. For instance, an 8th-level warmage with 18 Intelligence gets a +7 bonus on the damage dealt by any spell that deals hit point damage.
data.normal: N A character's warmage edge is equal to his Intelligence modifier.

data.name: Extra Exhalation
data.description: You can use your breath weapon one more time per day than normal.
data.prerequisite: Dragonblood subtype, breath weapon with limited uses per day.
data.benefit: You can use your breath weapon one additional time per day. The interval you must wait between breaths is 1d4 rounds.
data.special: You can gain this feat multiple times. Each time you take it, you can breathe one additional time per day.

data.name: Extra Familiar
data.description: You have an additional familiar.
data.benefit: By taking this feat you get an additional animal familiar. This familiar can be the same type of animal as your first, or a different type. Existing familiar feats apply to the new familiar. So if you have the Construct Familiar feat, for example, the new familiar you call can be a construct. The sorcerer or wizard gains all the benefits of both familiars, but identical bonuses from multiple familiars don't stack. For example, a wizard with both an owl and a cat familiar receives only a +2 bonus to Move Silently checks, not a +4 bonus.
data.special: A character can gain this feat multiple times. Each time you take the feat, you can call another familiar.

data.name: Extra Favored Enemy
data.description: You select an additional favored enemy
data.prerequisite: Base attack bonus +5, at least one favored enemy
data.benefit: You add an extra favored enemy to your list (see Table 3-14 in the Player's Handbook) beyond your normal allotment. Initially, you gain the standard +2 bonus on damage and the usual skill checks against this new favored enemy. When you advance beyond the level at which you gained Extra Favored Enemy, this bonus increases in the same way other favored enemy bonuses do. For example, suppose you select goblinoids as your first favored enemy when you are a 1stlevel ranger and magical beasts as your second when you reach 5th level. You choose to allocate your extra +2 bonus against goblinoids, giving you a +4 bonus against goblinoids and a +2 bonus against magical beasts. Then you take Extra Favored Enemy as your feat at 6th level and select aberrations. At this point, you have a +4 bonus against goblinoids and a +2 bonus against both magical beasts and aberrations. When you reach 10th level, you choose monstrous humanoids as your new favored enemy and allocate your extra +2 to goblinoids. Now your bonuses are +6 against goblinoids and +2 against magical beasts, aberrations, and monstrous humanoids.

data.name: Extra Followers
data.description: Your charismatic magnetism attracts even more followers to your banner.
data.benefit: You can lead twice as many followers as indicated for your Leadership score . For example, a character with this feat and a leadership score of 15 can lead forty 1st-level followers, four 2nd-level followers, and two 3rd-level followers.

data.name: Extra Granted Maneuver
data.description: You are especially devout or insightful, and you have more control over which of your martial maneuvers are currently granted than other crusaders.
data.benefit: You begin each encounter with one additional readied maneuver granted. This feat also applies when you determine a new set of granted maneuvers after recovering expended maneuvers. For example, if you are a 5th-level crusader, you normally begin an encounter or finish recovering expended maneuvers with two of your five readied maneuvers granted, and the remaining three maneuvers withheld. With this feat, three of your five readied maneuvers are granted, and, only two are withheld. Naturally, this benefit provides you with a better array of maneuver options early in a battle.
data.special: You can take this feat only once.

data.name: Extra Invocation
data.description: You learn an additional invocation.
data.benefit: You learn one additional invocation from the list available to you, choosing an invocation of one grade lower than the highest grade of invocation you know. For example, a 6th-level warlock could learn a least invocation, while a 16th-level warlock could learn any least, lesser, or greater invocation.
data.special: You can gain this feat multiple times. Each time, you gain an extra invocation of any grade (least, lesser, or greater) up to one lower than the highest grade of invocation you can currently use.

data.name: Extra Item Space
data.description: You can wear more magic items than are normally allowed.
data.benefit: You can wear one additional magic item beyond the normal limit for the item type in question. The multiple limbs you have must correspond to the limb on which that item type is normally worn. For example, a character is normally limited to two rings, but a marilith (with six arms) could wear three rings with this feat. Likewise, an ettin (with two heads) could wear two periapts or necklaces instead of the one normally allowed.
data.normal: Usually only one or two magic items that are restricted to a particular body part can be used at once.
data.special: You can take this feat more than once. Each time, it adds a new item space. You cannot have more item spaces than you have limbs or body parts of the appropriate kind.

data.name: Extra Music
data.description: You can use your bardic music more often than you otherwise could.
data.benefit: You can use your bardic music four extra times per day.
data.normal: Bards without the Extra Music feat can use bardic music once per day per bard level.
data.special: You can gain this feat multiple times. Its effects stack.

data.name: Extra Power
data.description: You can learn one more power.
data.benefit: You learn one additional power at any level up to one level lower than the highest-level power you can manifest. Thus, a 4th-level psion gains a new 0-level or 1st-level power, expanding his repertoire. A 4th-level psychic warrior can learn an extra 0-level power. You learn extra powers from your class power list.
data.special: You can gain this feat multiple times. Each time, you learn a new power at any level up to one less than the highest-level power you can manifest.

data.name: Extra Rage
data.description: You may rage more frequently than normal.
data.benefit: You rage or frenzy two more times per day than you otherwise could.
data.special: You can take this feat multiple times. Its effects stack.

data.name: Extra Readied Maneuver
data.description: You are an unusually perspicacious student of the Sublime Way, and you find it easy to keep a large number of ready for use.
data.benefit: Increase the number of maneuvers you can ready for your swordsage levels by one. For example, a 5th-level swordsage can normally ready six maneuvers. With this feat, he can ready seven.
data.special: You can take this feat only once.

data.name: Extra Remove Disease
data.description: You can remove disease more frequently than normal
data.prerequisite: Ability to cast remove disease as a spell-like ability.
data.benefit: Each time you take this feat, you can use your remove disease spell-like ability two more times per week than normal.
data.special: You can take this feat multiple times. Its effects stack.

data.name: Extra Rings
data.description: Your familiarity with forging magic rings allows you to make use of more rings than normal.
data.benefit: You can wear up to four magic rings, two on each hand, and all function normally.
data.normal: Without this feat, you can only wear and use two magic rings at one time.

data.name: Extra Shifter Trait
data.description: You manifest a second shifter trait while shifting.
data.benefit: Select a second shifter trait from those described in the Shifter Traits section (page 151, Monster Manual III). You manifest all the benefits of the second trait except for the temporary bonus to an ability score.

data.name: Extra Silence
data.description: You can generate a field of silence more often than other whisper gnomes can.
data.benefit: You can use your racial silence spell-like ability a number of times per day equal to 3 + your Charisma bonus.
data.normal: A whisper gnome can normally use his silence spell-like ability once per day.
data.special: You can take this feat more than once. Each time you take it after the first, you gain three additional uses of your silence ability per day.

data.name: Extra Slot
data.description: You can cast an additional spell.
data.benefit: You gain one extra spell slot in your daily allotment, at any level up to one lower than the highest level of spell you can currently cast. For example, a 4th-level sorcerer (maximum spell level 2nd) gains either an extra 0-level or 1st-level slot, and is able to cast any spell he knows of the chosen level one more time each day. likewise, a 4th-level wizard can prepare any extra 0-level or 1st-level spell he knows. Once selected, the extra spell slot never changes level.
data.special: You can gain this feat multiple times. Each time, you gain an extra spell slot at any level up to one lower than the highest level of spell you can currently cast.

data.name: Extra Smiting
data.description: You can make more smite attacks.
data.benefit: When you take this feat, you gain two extra attempts to smite per day Use whatever smite ability you have (the paladin's smite evil ability or the hunter of the dead's ability to smite undead, for example).
data.special: You can take this feat multiple times. Its effects stack

data.name: Extra Spell
data.description: You learn an additional spell.
data.benefit: You learn one additional spell at any level up to one lower than the highest level of spell you can currently cast. Thus, a 4th-level sorcerer (maximum spell level 2nd) gains a new 0-level or 1st-level spell known with which to expand her repertoire. For classes such as wizard that have more options for learning spells, Extra Spell is generally used to learn a specific spell that the character lacks access to and would be unable to research.
data.special: You can gain this feat multiple times. Each time, you learn a new spell at any level up to one lower than the highest level of spell you can cast.

data.name: Extra Spell Secret
data.description: You learn an additional spell secret.
data.prerequisite: Spell secret class ability, able to cast 2nd-level spells.
data.benefit: You choose one spell known to you that becomes permanently modified as though affected by Enlarge Spell, Extend Spell, Still Spell, or Silent Spell. The spell's level does not change, nor does the choice of spell and modification once chosen. As you go up in level, you can choose the same spell to be modified in different ways with multiple spell secrets (either from additional uses of this feat or through the spell secret class ability). You do not need to have the metamagic feat that you apply to the spell.
data.special: You can take this feat multiple times. Each time, you can select another spell to be permanently modified as though affected by one of the metamagic feats mentioned above. You can choose the same spell to be modified with multiple applications of this feat.

data.name: Extra Stunning
data.description: You gain extra stunning attacks.
data.benefit: You gain the ability to make three extra stunning attacks per day.
data.special: You can take this feat multiple times. Its effects stack.

data.name: Extra Stunning Attacks
data.description: You gain extra stunning attacks when fighting unarmed
data.benefit: You gain the ability to make three extra stunning attacks per day. You may take this feat multiple times.

data.name: Extra Tricks
data.description: A creature with this feat can learn more tricks than normal.
data.prerequisite: Animal or magical beast with Int 1 or 2, must know at least one trick.
data.benefit: The creature can learn three more tricks than normal.
data.normal: Without this feat, animals and magical beasts can learn a maximum of three tricks per point of Intelligence.
data.special: This feat can be taken multiple times. Each time it is taken, the creature can learn up to three more tricks.

data.name: Extra Turning
data.description: You can turn or rebuke creatures more often than normal.
data.prerequisite: Ability to turn or rebuke creatures.
data.benefit: Each time you take this feat, you can use your ability to turn or rebuke creatures four more times per day than normal.If you have the ability to turn or rebuke more than one creature (such as a good-aligned cleric with access to the Fire domain, who can turn undead and water creatures and can rebuke fire creatures), each of your turning or rebuking abilities gains four additional uses per day.
data.normal: Without this feat, a character can typically turn or rebuke undead (or other creatures) a number of times per equal to 3 + his or her Charisma modifier.
data.special: You can gain Extra Turning multiple times. Its effects stack. Each time you take the feat, you can use each of your turning or rebuking abilities four additional times per day.

data.name: Extra Unique Power
data.description: If you take this feat, you can manifest one more unique power per day.
data.prerequisite: Possess the unique powers per day class ability.
data.benefit: Choose a power level from which you can already manifest powers (1st through 9th). From now on, you can manifest one more unique power per day from among the powers you know of this level.
data.special: You can take this feat multiple times however, you can't take more than once per character level.

data.name: Extra Wild Shape
data.description: You can use wild shape more frequently than you normally could.
data.benefit: You may use your wild shape ability two more times per day than you otherwise could. If you are able to use wild shape to become an elemental, you also gain one additional use per day of your elemental wild shape.
data.special: You can take this feat multiple times, gaining two additional wild shapes of your usual type and one additional elemental wild shape (if you have this capability) each time.

data.name: Extract Demonic Essence
data.description: You can draw upon the living essence of a willing or captured demon to fuel the creation of items or the casting of potent spells.
data.prerequisite: Any two item creation feats, Demon Mastery.
data.benefit: Whenever you need to expend experience points to craft a magic item or cast a spell with an XP component, you can draw upon the life force of a nearby demon to reduce the XP cost to you. Before beginning the process, you must secure a demon whose Hit Dice equal or exceed the caster level of the item or spell in question. The demon could be one that you summoned with a planar ally spell, or one that you have bound with a planar binding spell, or even one that you simply encountered and convinced to aid you. The demon need not be willing, but it must remain within 30 feet of you for the duration of your work on the item or the casting time of the spell, and you must have line of effect (but not necessarily line of sight) to it during that time.Drawing essence from a demon does not increase the time required to craft the item or cast the spell, but the process is draining for both you and the demon. Each of you rakes 1d6 points of Constitution damage when the essence is channeled from the demon into your body, then converted into magical energy.An unwilling demon can attempt a Fortitude save (DC 10 + 1/2 your character level + your Cha modifier) to resist removal of its essence. Success negates your use of this feat and forces you to either pay the full cost yourself or abort the creation or casting.Successfully drawing essence from a demon in this way reduces the XP cost of the spell or item creation by one-half, but it complicates the process. If you use this feat to reduce the XP cost of a spell, you must succeed on a Concentration check (DC 20 + the demon's HD) to cast the spell. If you use this feat to offset the XP cost of crafting a magic item, the demon's essence has a 10% chance to corrupt the item in some way, imbuing it with a curse. (The DM determines the nature of this curse randomly by rolling on the tables on pages 272-273 of the Dungeon Master's Guide.)

data.name: Extraordinary Artisan
data.description: You are an expert at creating magic items at a lower cost than usual.
data.benefit: When determining the gold piece cost in raw materials you need to craft any item, reduce the base price by 25%.

data.name: Extraordinary Concentration
data.description: Your mind is so focused that you can cast spells even while concentrating on another spell.
data.benefit: When concentrating to maintain a spell, you can make a Concentration check (DC 25 + spell level) to maintain concentration with just a move action. If you bear the DC by 10 or more, you can maintain concentration on the spell as a swift action. Using this ability is a free action, but if you fail the Concentration check, you lose concentration on the maintained spell and its effect ends. This feat does not give you the ability to maintain concentration on more than one spell at a time.
data.normal: Concentrating on a spell is a standard action.

data.name: Extraordinary Spell Aim
data.description: You can shape a spell's area to exclude one creature from its effects.
data.benefit: Whenever you cast a spell with an area, you can attempt to shape the spell's area so that one creature within the area is unaffected by the spell. To accomplish this, you must succeed on a Spellcraft check (DC 25 + spell level).Casting a spell affected by the Extraordinary Spell Aim feat requires a full-round action unless the spell's normal casting time is longer, in which case the casting time is unchanged.

data.name: Extraordinary Trapsmith
data.description: You are an expert at constructing mechanical traps.
data.benefit: When you determine the cost of raw materials and the time required to create a mechanical trap, reduce the base price by 25%.
data.special: A kobold who selects this feat can instead reduce the base price by 50% for determining raw materials and time required to create a mechanical trap.

data.name: Eye For Talent
