data.description: You can use your voice to shatter ice.
data.benefit: By expending one of your daily uses of bardic music, you can cause any unattended nonmagical ice object or portion of ice to explode violently. The target must be within 30 feet, and you must be able to sing in order to shatter the ice. Any creature located in the 5-foot square in which you target with this ability takes piercing damage equal to 2d6 + your Charisma modifier. Creatures made of ice that you specifically target with this effect take double damage.

data.name: Ice Troll Berserker
data.description: When raging, your skin becomes very thick and tough like the ice trolls that plague parts of your homeland.
data.prerequisite: Constitution 13, ability to rage, membership in the Ice Troll berserker lodge (see Berserker Lodges). Region: Rashemen
data.benefit: When raging, you gain a +2 natural armor bonus to AC. During a greater rage this natural armor bonus increases to +4.

data.name: Icy Calling
data.description: Your summon spells work better in the frostfell if you summon native creatures.
data.benefit: In cold areas (temperature at or below 40°F), creatures native to cold environments and creatures with the cold subtype summoned by you are more powerful and agile than normal. The summoned creatures gain a +4 enhancement bonus to Strength and Dexterity for the duration of the spell that summoned them.In areas of extreme cold (below -20°F), any summoned creature affected by this feat has maximum hit points when summoned.

data.name: Ignore Material Components
data.description: You need not use any material components in casting your spells.
data.prerequisite: Eschew Materials, Spellcraft 25 ranks, ability to cast 9th-level arcane or divine spells
data.benefit: You may cast your spells without any material components. This feat does not affect the need for a focus or divine focus.

data.name: Ignore Special Requirements
data.description: The strange constraints that vestiges place on their summoning are meaningless to you.
data.benefit: You can ignore the special requirements of vestiges with which you make pacts.

data.name: Ignore Turn Resistance
data.description: Your ability to affect undead with channeled energy is powerful enough to penetrate resistance to turning.
data.prerequisite: Cha 19, Empower Turning, Extra Turning, ability to turn or rebuke creatures.
data.benefit: Whenever you attempt to turn, rebuke, control, or destroy undead, their turn resistance (if any) is effectively reduced by a number equal to your Charisma modifier (to a minimum turn resistance of +0) for that attempt only.

data.name: Ildritch Corruption
data.description: You can add power to your spells or spell-like abilities at the expense of your companions' health.
data.prerequisite: Any one metamagic feat, moderate depravity.
data.benefit: You can enlarge, extend, heighten, or widen a spell, as though you had the appropriate metamagic feat, without increasing the spell level or, for spontaneous casters, casting time. You can apply any number of these metamagic effects to a spell in this way; if you choose heighten, then that spell's level is heightened by up to two levels.For each level of metamagic you apply, one of your allies takes 2 points of Constitution damage. In this case, "ally" is defined as someone who knowingly and willingly fights alongside you against a common foe, or who otherwise considers you a trusted companion. You decide, when casting the spell, which ally takes the Constitution damage. You cannot select an ally immune to Constitution damage or an ally without a Constitution score. For purposes of this spell, you cannot designate a summoned or charmed being as your ally.You can use this feat three times per day.
data.special: You can select this feat more than once. Each time you do, you gain an additional three uses per day.

data.name: Illiterate
data.description: You cannot read, but you have devoted yourself to learning other skills.
data.benefit: Choose any one skill except Decipher Script or Forgery. You gain a +1 bonus on checks using that skill. Drawback: You are illiterate.
data.special: You can eliminate the negative effect of this trait by spending 2 skill points to become literate. Unlike with the barbarian, you cannot become literate by taking a level in any class other than barbarian.You can't select this trait if your character is already illiterate because of race, class, or any other reason.

data.name: Illithid Blast
data.description: You can convert your psionic energy into a mind blast.
data.prerequisite: Illithid Heritage, two other illithid feats, manifester level 5th.
data.benefit: Once per day as a standard action that requires the expenditure of your psionic focus, you can channel power points into a mind blast, which is a psi-like attack in a 15-foot cone. Anyone caught in the cone must succeed on a Will save (DC 10 + 1/2 your HD + your Cha modifier) or be stunned for 1d4 rounds.

data.name: Illithid Bloodline
data.description: Illithids frequently perform experiments on their slaves, grafting and splicing bits from one species onto another. Often, illithids use body parts from their own race to augment their slaves. Eventually, Some of these enhanced slaves escape back to their own people. You are the descendant of one such escaped slave.Creatures with the Illithid Bloodline feat tend toward no particular alignment, personality trait, or overall outlook. They do, however, typically share certain physical traits, such as lacking hair but possessing black eyes and pale skin with a slightly purplish tint.Sorcerers who have an escaped illithid slave in their ancestry tend to select spells that manipulate or ruin the mind. They favor enchantment spells above all others.
data.prerequisite: Ability to cast arcane spells without preparation.
data.benefit: Your ancestry gives you a bonus spell known at each spell level, starting at first, from the following list.

data.name: Illithid Compulsion
data.description: You can call upon your heritage and enhance your ability to manipulate the minds of other creatures.
data.benefit: Your manifester level for compulsion powers increases by one. You also add 1 to the save DCs of all psionic powers you manifest that have the compulsion descriptor.

data.name: Illithid Enthusiast
data.description: When you manipulate the minds of other creatures, you are heartened and emboldened by your success.
data.benefit: After you successfully affect a foe with a compulsion power, you gain a morale bonus equal to one-half the number of power points spent on the power on the next Concentration, Knowledge (Psionics), Psicraft, or Sense Motive check you make before the end of the next round.

data.name: Illithid Extraction
data.description: Your acceptance of your illithid heritage is so encompassing that you have learned how to extract the brain of a helpless victim.
data.benefit: You have fully embraced your illithid heritage and have developed a taste for the brains of other living creatures. You can extract the brain of a helpless or stunned victim once you have established a hold on its head with all four of your tentacles. You must begin your turn with all four tentacles attached. As a full-round action that provokes attacks of opportunity, you must then make a grapple check. If your grapple check is successful, your victim takes 2d4 points of Intelligence damage and grants you a number of temporary power points equal to the amount of intelligence damage you deal. Any creature reduced to Intelligence 0 by this attack is instantly slain.Temporary power points gained with this feat last for 1 hour, and power points granted by this feat do not stack with each other.This ability is useless against constructs, elementals, oozes, plants, and undead. It is not instantly fatal to opponents with multiple heads, such as ettins and hydras.

data.name: Illithid Grapple
data.description: You embrace more of your illithid heritage, and grow at least one long purplish tentacle that you can reveal and unfurl when you open your mouth.
data.prerequisite: Illithid Heritage, one other illithid feat.
data.benefit: You gain a natural tentacle attack. If you can use weapons, you retain that ability, as well as any other natural weapons you have. If you are fighting without weapons, you can use your tentacle or another natural weapon as a primary attack. If you are armed with a weapon, you can use your tentacle or your weapon as a primary attack. If you make a full attack, you can use the weapon as your primary attack along with your tentacle as a natural secondary attack. The tentacle does not improve your natural reach.Your tentacle deals 1d4 points of damage, and you can use your Dexterity modifier in place of your Strength modifier on attacks with your tentacle if you have the Weapon Finesse feat.Additionally, you gain the improved grab special ability that you can use only with your tentacle, allowing you to initiate a grapple as a free action, without provoking attacks of opportunity, each time you hit with a tentacle attack. You can only attempt to grapple creatures within one size category of you with your tentacle.
data.special: You can take this feat up to four times. Each time you do so, you gain an additional tentacle. Once you have gained all four tentacles, the lower half of your face transforms to resemble that of an illithid.As a full-round action, you can give up any other attacks you have to attack with as many tentacles you possess, as described above, and can attempt to initiate a separate grapple with each tentacle as a free action. You do not gain cumulative bonuses for each tentacle already attached (as an illithid would).

data.name: Illithid Heritage
data.description: Somewhere in the deeps of time, your bloodline was polluted with illithid influence. Recognizing this taint but deciding to make the best of it, you have decided to embrace your connection with your illithid bloodline for the power it can offer you.
data.prerequisite: Power point reserve of 1 or more.
data.benefit: You gain Intimidate as a class skill. In addition, you gain a bonus on saving throws against spells, powers, spell-like abilities, and psi-like abilities. This bonus is equal to one-half the number of illithid feats you have, including this one (round down, minimum 1).

data.name: Illithid Legacy
data.description: You have realized greater psionic power through your illithid heritage.
data.prerequisite: Illithid Heritage, one other illithid feat, manifester level 3rd.
data.benefit: You know more powers because of the illithid heritage you possess. Add psionic charm and read thoughts to your list of powers known.

data.name: Illithid Legacy, Greater
data.description: Your knowledge of psionic power has grown even further due to your illithid heritage.
data.prerequisite: Illithid Legacy, any one other illithid feat, manifester level 7th.
data.benefit: You know more powers because of your illithid heritage. Add dispel psionics and psionic dominate to your list of powers known.

data.name: Illithid Skin
data.description: Your skin takes on the glistening, rubbery, green-mauve consistency of your illithid parentage.
data.benefit: Your natural armor bonus increases by 1.
data.special: You can take this feat up to three times. Each time, your natural armor bonus increases by another 1.

data.name: Imbue Weapon
data.description: Your channeled energy lets you strike a particular foe with greater power.
data.prerequisite: Extra Turning, the ability to turn or rebuke undead.
data.benefit: As a standard action, you may imbue your weapon with divine wrath and designate a target opponent. Whenever you attack the designated opponent with the imbued weapon, you gain a bonus on your attack and damage rolls equal to +1 per four cleric levels you possess (minimum +1). The weapon remains imbued for a number of rounds equal to the bonus you grant it.

data.name: Imbued Defense
data.description: Your staff protects its master, deflecting attacks targeting you.
data.prerequisite: Caster level 1st, ability to imbue a staff
data.benefit: While casting a spell on the defensive and in contact with your imbued staff, add your Wisdom modifier to your AC until your next action, in addition to your Dexterity modifier.
data.special: If your imbued staff is ever destroyed, this benefit is lost until a new staff is imbued. Preparing a staff to receive this feat requires 12 hours and expends materials valued at 500 gp.

data.name: Imbued Healing
data.description: You imbue your healing spells with additional benefits based on the power of your belief.
data.prerequisite: Ability to cast conjuration (healing) spells; access to one or more domains.
data.benefit: Whenever you cast a 1st-level or higher conjuration (healing) spell, you not only heal your subject of hit point damage, but you also confer on it a carrier effect deriving from a domain to which you have access. If you have access to more than one of the following domains, choose which carrier effect to use each time you use this ability.Each of these carrier effects has a duration of 1 minute per level of the conjuration (healing) spell cast and an equivalent spell level.If you are using domains from publications other than the Player's Handbook, use the Domains From Publications Other Than The Player's Handbook sidebar on page 53 (Complete Champion)to determine an appropriate domain equivalency.

data.name: Imbued Strength
data.description: Your will can overcome the limitations of your physical abilities.
data.prerequisite: Caster level 3rd, ability to imbue a staff.
data.benefit: When you successfully hit with your imbued staff, use your Wisdom modifier to determine your damage bonus, rather than your Strength modifier.
data.special: If your imbued staff is ever destroyed, this feat is lost until a new staff is imbued. Preparing a staff to receive this feat requires 12 hours and expends materials valued at 500 gp.

data.name: Imbued Summoning
data.description: Your summoning spells gain an element of surprise. You can summon creatures that come into existence with the benefit of a spell such as invisibility or bull's strength.
data.benefit: When you cast a spell from the summoning subschool, you can choose to grant the summoned creature the benefit of any spell of 3rd level or lower you can cast that has a range of touch. You cast the spell you wish to grant the creature (using a prepared spell or a spell slot) at the same time you cast your summoning spell. The creature gains the benefit of the spell when it appears.An imbued summoning spell uses up a spell slot one level higher than the spell's actual level.

data.name: Impeding Attack
data.description: Your strikes render your target temporarily clumsy and awkward.
data.benefit: Your successful sneak attack imposes a -2 penalty on the target's Strength and Dexterity checks, as well as on any Strength- and Dexterity-based skill checks, for 10 rounds. If you use this feat a second time on a target before 10 rounds have elapsed, the effect of the first use expires.Using this feat reduces your sneak attack damage by 3d6.

data.name: Imperious Command
data.description: You strike deep and abiding terror in your foes.
data.benefit: If you successfully demoralize a foe in combat, the foe cowers in fear for 1 round and is shaken in the following round.

data.name: Imprint Epic Stone
data.description: See Scribe Epic Scroll
data.benefit: See Scribe Epic Scroll

data.name: Imprint Stone
data.description: You can create power stones to store psionic powers.
data.benefit: You can create a power stone of any power that you know. Encoding a power stone takes one day for each 1,000 gp in its base price. The base price of a power stone is the level of the stored power x its manifester level X 25 gp. To imprint a power stone, you must spend 1/25 of this base price in XP and use up raw materials costing one-half of this base price.Any power stone that stores a power with an XP cost also carries a commensurate cost. In addition to the costs derived from the base price, you must pay the XP when encoding the stone.

data.name: Improve Disguise
data.description: You can more effectively disguise yourself in a specific way, such as changing your gender, race, age, or social class.
data.prerequisite: Cha 13, Disguise 7 ranks, Sense Motive 5 ranks.
data.benefit: You do not suffer the -2 penalty for attempting to disguise yourself as another gender, race, or age category. You can also don a disguise in half the normal amount of time.

data.name: Improved Aid
data.description: You are descended from Hida Tadaka, the great Crab daimyo who gave his life to avert a war between his clan and the Lion. Clan: Clan: Crab.
data.benefit: When using the Aid Another action in melee combat (see Aid Another in the Player's Handbook), your ally gains a +4 circumstance bonus on his attack roll or to his AC.
data.normal: The Aid Another action normally gives a +2 circumstance bonus on your ally's attack roll or AC against a single opponent.

data.name: Improved Aid Another
data.description: You are skilled at aiding allies both on and off the battlefield.
data.benefit: When successfully using the aid another action, you grant your ally a +4 bonus.
data.normal: Without this feat, you only give your ally a +2 bonus when using the aid another action.
data.special: A thane may select Improved Aid Another as one of his fighter bonus feats.

data.name: Improved Alignment-based Casting
data.description: Your spells of a particular alignment are more powerful than normal.
data.prerequisite: Access to domain of Chaos, Evil, Good, or law, alignment must match domain chosen, ability to cast 9th-level divine spells
data.benefit: Select an alignment-based domain (Chaos, Evil, Good, or law) to which you have access. You cast spells with that alignment descriptor at +3 caster level.
data.special: This benefit overrides (does not stack with) the granted powers of the Chaos, Evil, Good, and Law domains. You may select this feat multiple times. Its effects do not stack. Each time you take the feat, it applies to a different alignment-based domain to which you have access.

data.name: Improved Animation
data.description: Your undead animations are unusually powerful.
data.benefit: Undead you create using animate dead or the create undead spells gain an additional Hit Die and a +1 competence bonus to attack and damage rolls. Note: Undead do not gain an increase in size due to the increased Hit Dice from this feat.

data.name: Improved Armor Dance
data.description: You at an expert at using armor to its best advantage
data.prerequisite: Base attack bonus +2, Armor Dance, Perform (dance) 2 ranks.
data.benefit: You may gain the dodge bonus provided by the Armor Dance feat while wearing Maztican medium armor or similarly decorated medium armor. If you are wearing hide armor the dodge bonus provided by the Armor Dance feat increases to +4.
data.special: A fighter may select Improved Armor Dance as one of his fighter bonus feats.

data.name: Improved Arrow Of Death
data.description: Your arrows of death are harder to resist.
data.prerequisite: Dex 19, Wis 19, Point Blank Shot, Precise Shot, arrow of death class feature
data.benefit: Add +2 to the DC of your arrows of death.
data.special: This feat may be taken multiple times. Its effects stack.

data.name: Improved Assume Supernatural Ability
data.description: You gain skill with using a supernatural ability of an assumed form.
data.prerequisite: Wis 17, Assume Supernatural Ability, ability to assume a new form magically.
data.benefit: This feat functions like Assume Supernatural Ability, except that you do not take the -2 penalty on all attack rolls, saves, skill checks, and ability checks when using it.
data.special: You can take this feat multiple times, but you cannot apply it more than once to the same special ability. Each time you take this feat, it applies to a different special ability.

data.name: Improved Aura of Courage
data.description: Your aura of courage is stronger than normal.
data.prerequisite: Cha 25, aura of courage class ability
data.benefit: Your aura of courage grants a +8 morale bonus on saving throws against fear effects.

data.name: Improved Aura of Despair
data.description: Your aura of despair is wider than normal.
data.prerequisite: Cha 25, aura of despair class ability
data.benefit: Your aura of despair causes a -4 morale penalty on all saving throws.

data.name: Improved Binding
data.description: You are so adept at binding vestiges that you can contact powerful ones more easily than other soul binders can.
data.benefit: Your effective binder level is 2 higher than normal for the purpose of determining the level of vestige you can bind. This benefit does not increase your binder level in any other circumstance.

data.name: Improved Buckler Defense
data.description: You can attack with an off-hand weapon while retaining a buckler's shield bonus to your Armor Class.
data.benefit: When you attack with a weapon in your off-hand, you may still apply your buckler's shield bonus to your Armor Class.
data.normal: Without this feat, a character wielding a buckler who attacks with an off-hand weapon loses the buckler's shield bonus to AC until his or her next turn.
data.special: A fighter may select Improved Buckler Defense as one of his fighter bonus feats.

data.name: Improved Bull Rush
data.description: You know how to push opponents back.
data.benefit: When you perform a bull rush, you do not draw an attack of opportunity from the defender. You also gain a +4 bonus on the opposed Strength check you make to push back the defender.

data.name: Improved Cohort
data.description: You attract a more powerful cohort than you normally would.
data.benefit: The maximum level of the cohort you gain from the leadership feat is one lower than your character level.
data.normal: Without this feat, a cohort's maximum level is two levels below the associated PC's level.

data.name: Improved Cold Endurance
data.description: Your training and natural hardiness have improved your natural resistance to cold temperatures.
data.prerequisite: Base Fortitude save bonus +6, Cold Endurance.
data.benefit: You gain resistance to cold 5. If you already have resistance to cold, it increases by 5 (maximum resistance to cold 15).
data.special: You can gain this feat multiple times; its effects stack. You cannot have more than resistance to cold 15 as a result of taking this feat.

data.name: Improved Combat Casting
data.description: You can cast spells while threatened without fear of being attacked.
data.benefit: You don't incur attacks of opportunity for casting spells when threatened.

data.name: Improved Combat Expertise
data.description: You have mastered the art of defense in combat.
data.prerequisite: Int 13, Combat Expertise, base attack bonus +6.
data.benefit: When you use the Combat Expertise feat to improve your Armor Class, the number you subtract from your attack roll and add to your AC can be any number that does not exceed your base attack bonus.
data.normal: With Combat Expertise, the number can be no greater than +5.
data.special: A fighter may select Improved Combat Expertise as one of his fighter bonus feats.

data.name: Improved Combat Manifestation
data.description: See Improved Combat Casting
data.benefit: See Improved Combat Casting

data.name: Improved Combat Reflexes
data.description: You react to any drop in your opponents' guard with lightning speed.
data.prerequisite: Dex 13, Combat Reflexes, base attack bonus +6.
data.benefit: For any given opportunity in melee combat, you can make two attacks of opportunity. The second attack is at a -5 penalty, just like your second normal attack in any given round. You still cannot exceed your normal maximum number of attacks of opportunity in a round.

data.name: Improved Combat Reflexes (Epic)
data.description: You can respond to any number of opponents who let their defenses down.
data.benefit: There is no limit to the number of attacks of opportunity you can make in one round. (You still can't make more than one attack of opportunity against a single person in a round.)

data.name: Improved Control Visage
data.description: You can change your ghost form's appearance.
data.benefit: You can change the appearance of your ghost body, clothing, armor, weapons, and equipment as a standard action as if using the alter self spell, although the changes are purely cosmetic and do not grant you any additional abilities (such as flight if you create wings). Thus, you could make yourself look like a hobgoblin ghost, an elf ghost, fat, thin, short, tall, and so on, although you still appear semitransparent.

data.name: Improved Cooperative Metamagic
data.description: Your ability to enhance an ally's spell during casting is expanded.
data.prerequisite: Spellcraft 30 ranks, Silent Spell, Still Spell, Quicken Spell, cooperative metamagic class feature.
data.benefit: You can use your cooperative metamagic ability to apply the Silent Spell, Still Spell, or Quicken Spell feats to your ally's spell. If you apply the Quicken Spell feat to the spell, both your action and the caster's are free actions. You must still ready an action to use this ability when your ally begins casting, but you can ready a different standard action as well.In addition, you can use your cooperative metamagic ability an extra two times per day.
data.normal: Without this feat, a character cannot use cooperative metamagic to apply the Silent Spell, Still Spell, or Quicken Spell feats to an ally's spell.

data.name: Improved Counterspell
data.description: You understand the nuances of magic to such an extent that you can counter your opponents' spells with great efficiency.
data.benefit: When counterspelling, you may use a spell of the same school that is one or more levels higher than the target spell.
data.normal: Without this feat, you may counter a spell only with the same spell or with a spell specifically designated as countering the target spell.

data.name: Improved Critical
data.description: Choose one type of weapon, such as longsword or greataxe. With that weapon, you know how to hit where it hurts.
data.prerequisite: Proficient with weapon, base attack bonus +8
data.benefit: When using the weapon you selected, your threat range is doubled. For example, a longsword usually threatens a critical on a 19-20 (two numbers). If a character using a longsword has Improved Critical (longsword), the threat range becomes 17-20 (four numbers).
data.special: You can gain this feat multiple times. The effects do not stack. Each time you take the feat, it applies to a new weapon.The effect does not stack with any other effect that expands the threat range of a weapon (such as the keen edge spell).

data.name: Improved Damage Reduction
data.description: You gain damage reduction or improve your existing damage reduction.
data.benefit: You gain damage reduction 1/adamantine or improve your existing damage reduction by 1.
data.special: If you have the Adamantine Body feat, you can take this feat multiple times.

data.name: Improved Darkvision
data.description: Your ability to see in the dark is greater than normal.
data.benefit: The range of your darkvision doubles. This feat does not stack with darkvision granted by magic items or non-permanent magical effects.
data.special: This feat may be taken multiple times. Its effects stack. Remember that two doublings equals a tripling, and so on.

data.name: Improved Death Attack
data.description: Your death attack is harder to overcome.
data.prerequisite: Death attack class feature, sneak attack +5d6
data.benefit: Add +2 to the DC of your death attack.
data.special: This feat may be taken multiple times. Its effects stack.

data.name: Improved Deflection
data.description: You are adept at deflecting things before they strike you.
data.benefit: Your incorporeal deflection bonus increases by 1. You cannot use this feat to increase the effect of a deflection bonus gained from a magic item or spell; it applies only to the deflection bonus derived from your Charisma score because you are a ghost.
data.special: You may take this feat multiple times. Its effects stack. When you are forced to manifest fully, this feat applies to the natural armor bonus granted by your Charisma.

data.name: Improved Disarm
data.description: You know how to disarm opponents in melee combat.
data.benefit: You do not provoke an attack of opportunity when you attempt to disarm an opponent, nor does the opponent have a chance to disarm you. You also gain a +4 bonus to the opposed attack roll you make to disarm your opponent.
data.normal: See Disarm rules
data.special: A monk may select Improved Disarm as a bonus feat at 6th level, even if she does not meet the prerequisites.

data.name: Improved Diversion
data.description: You can create a diversion to hide quickly and with less effort.
data.benefit: You can use Bluff to create a diversion to hide (see page 68 of the Player's Handbook) as a move action. You gain a +4 bonus on Bluff checks made for this purpose.
data.normal: Without this feat, creating a diversion to hide using the Bluff skill requires a standard action.
data.special: A fighter may select Improved Diversion as one of his fighter bonus feats.

data.name: Improved Domination
data.description: The creature can dominate all manner of monsters.
data.prerequisite: Cha 21, Intimidate 18 ranks, dominate person supernatural ability.
data.benefit: Your dominate ability applies to creatures of any type.

data.name: Improved Dragon Wings
data.description: Your draconic wings now grant you flight.
data.benefit: You can fly at a speed of 30 feet (average maneuverability). You can't fly while carrying a medium or heavy load or while fatigued or exhausted.You can safely fly for a number of consecutive rounds equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum 1 round). You can exert yourself to fly for up to ice as long, but then you're fatigued at the end of the flight. You are likewise fatigued after spending a total of more than 10 minutes per day flying. Because you can glide before, after, and between rounds of actual flight, you can remain aloft for extended periods, even if you can only use flight for 1 round at a time without becoming fatigued.When you reach 12 HD, you have enough stamina and prowess to fly for longer periods. You can fly at a speed of 30 feet (average maneuverability), and flying requires no more exertion than than walking or running.You can make a dive attack. A dive attack works like a charge, but you must fly a minimum of 30 feet and descend at least 10 feet. You can make a dive attack only when wielding a piercing weapon; if the attack hits, it deals double damage.You can use the run action while flying, provided you fly in a straight line.

data.name: Improved Element Focus
data.description: The spells of your specialized element are even more powerful than normal.
data.benefit: The spells of your chosen element focus are treated as if you had the Greater Spell Focus feat; that is, their save DC is increased by a total of +4 (including the benefit from Spell Focus).

data.name: Improved Elemental Heritage
data.description: You have manifested an even stronger tie to your elemental ancestor, resulting in a minor resistance to elemental effects.
data.prerequisite: Air Heritage, Earth Heritage, Fire Heritage, or Water Heritage.
data.benefit: You gain resistance 5 to a specific type of energy, based on your elemental heritage: acid (earth), cold (water), electricity (air), or fire (fire).You also gain a +2 bonus on saving throws against spells with the descriptor that matches your chosen elemental heritage (air, earth, fire, or water).

data.name: Improved Elemental Wild Shape
data.description: You can take the form of a greater variety of elementals than normal.
data.prerequisite: Wis 25, ability to wild shape into an elemental
data.benefit: Your ability to wild shape into an elemental is expanded to include all elemental creatures (not just air, earth, fire, and water elementals) of any size that you can take when wild shaping into an animal. For instance, if you are normally capable of taking the shape of an animal of Huge size, you can now wild shape into a Huge elemental creature. You gain all extraordinary and supernatural abilities of the elemental whose form you take.
data.normal: Without this feat, you may only take the shape of a Small, Medium, or Large air, earth, fire, or water elemental.

data.name: Improved Encumbrance
data.description: You can carry more than your Strength would normally allow.
data.benefit: Your encumbrance and carrying capacity is determined (from the chart on page 142 of the Player's Handbook) as though your Strength was 4 points higher than it is. For example, if your strength is 7, this Feat would allow you to carry up to 38 lb. as a light load (as though your Strength were 11).

data.name: Improved Energy Drain
data.description: You draw extra power from your energy-drained victims.
data.benefit: Whenever you bestow a negative level upon a creature, you gain a +1 bonus on skill checks, ability checks, attack rolls, and saving throws for 1 hour.

data.name: Improved Energy Resistance
data.description: Choose one form of energy to which you have a natural (not spell- or item-granted) resistance. Your inherent resistance to this kind of energy is more effective than normal.
data.prerequisite: Naturally resistant to a form of energy (acid, cold, electricity, fire, sonic)
data.benefit: Your resistance to that type of energy increases by 1.For example, if you are an aasimar, you normally have acid, cold, and electricity resistance 5. You could use this feat to increase one of those resistances to 10; the other two are unaffected.

data.name: Improved Essentia Capacity
data.description: Your capability of investing essentia improves.
data.benefit: The essentia capacity of your incarnum feats improves by 1, up to a maximum value equal to your Constitution bonus.

data.name: Improved Familiar
data.description: So long as you are able to acqure a new familiar, you may choose you new familiar from a non-standard list. (see Familiars)
data.prerequisite: Ability to acquire a new familiar, compatible alignment.
data.benefit: When choosing a familiar, non-standard familiars are also available to the spellcaster. The spellcaster may choose a familiar with an alignment up to one step away on each of the alignment axes (lawful through chaotic, good through evil). For example, a chaotic good spellcaster could acquire a neutral familiar.The improved familiar is magicall linksed to its master just like a normal familiar. The improved familiar uses the basic statistics for a creature of its kind, with these exceptions:Hit Points: One-half master's total or the familiar's normal total, whichever is higher.Attacks: Use the master's base attack bonus or the familiar's, whichever is higher. Use the familiar's Dexterity or Strength modifier, whichever is greater, to get the familiar's attack bonus with unarmed attacks. Damage equals that of a normal creature of that kind.Special Attacks: The familiar has the special attacks of its kind.Special Qualities: The familiar has all the special qualities of its kind.Saving Throws: The familiar uses its master's save bonuses if they are better than the familiar's.Skills: Use the normal skills for a creature of its kind.Familiar Special Abilities: see Familiars.

data.name: Improved Favored Enemy
data.description: You know how to hit your favored enemies where it hurts.
data.prerequisite: Favored enemy ability, base attack bonus +5
data.benefit: You deal an extra 3 points of damage to your favored enemies. This benefit stacks with any existing favored enemy bonus gained from another class.

data.name: Improved Favored Enemy (Epic)
data.description: You know how to hit your favored enemies where it hurts.
data.benefit: Add +1 to the bonus on Bluff, Listen, Sense Motive, Spot, and Survival checks and damage rolls against all your favored enemies.
data.special: This feat may be taken multiple times. Its effects stack.

data.name: Improved Feint
data.description: Your are skilled at misdirecting your opponent's attention on combat.
data.benefit: You can make a Bluff check to feint in combat as a move action.
data.normal: Feinting in combat is a standard action.

data.name: Improved Fiendish Servant
data.description: You gain the service of a powerful fiendish animal servitor.
data.benefit: Add the following creatures to your list of eligible fiendish servants: ape, black bear, boar, crocodile, dire badger, dire bat, dire weasel, heavy warhorse, leopard, monitor lizard, constrictor snake, Large viper snake, wolverine. In addition, any fiendish servant you call has the fiendish creature template (Monster Manual page 107). The fiendish servant otherwise conforms to the rules in the Dungeon Master's Guide (page 183).

data.name: Improved Flight
data.description: You gain greater maneuverability when flying than you would normally have.
data.prerequisite: Ability to fly (naturally, magically, or through shapechanging).
data.benefit: Your maneuverability class while flying improves by one step-clumsy to poor, poor to average, average to good, or good to perfect.

data.name: Improved Flyby Attack
data.description: You can attack on the wing with increased mobility.
data.prerequisite: Fly speed, Dodge, Flyby Attack, Mobility.
data.benefit: When flying, you can take a move action (including a dive) and another partial action at any point during the move. If this partial action is an attack, you incur no attacks of opportunity for moving through areas threatened by your target. You cannot take a second move action during a round when you make a flyby attack.
data.normal: Without this feat, you can take a partial action either before or after your move. Even with the Flyby Attack feat, you incur attacks of opportunity for moving through areas threatened by the target of your flyby attack.

data.name: Improved Fortification
data.description: You improve your warforged fortification, gaining immunity to sneak attacks and extra damage from critical hits.
data.benefit: You gain immunity to sneak attacks and extra damage from critical hits, but you lose the ability to be healed by spells of the healing subschool.

data.name: Improved Frosty Touch
data.description: Your frosty touch causes more cold damage. Region: Uldra.
data.benefit: Your frosty touch ability now deals 2 points of cold damage.
data.normal: Your frosty touch ability deals 1 point of cold damage.

data.name: Improved Ghost Flight
data.description: Your ghost body can fly rapidly.
data.benefit: Your ghost fly speed increases by 10 feet, and you can use the run action when you fly.

data.name: Improved Grapple
data.description: You are skilled in martial arts that emphasize holds and throws.
data.benefit: You do not incur an attack of opportunity when you make a touch attack to start a grapple. You also gain a +4 bonus on all grapple checks (regardless of whether you started the grapple).
data.normal: Characters without this feat incur an attack of opportunity when they make a touch attack to start a grapple.
data.special: A monk may select Improved Grapple as a bonus feat at 1st level, even if she does not meet the prerequisites.

data.name: Improved Grapple (OA)
data.description: You are skilled in martial arts that emphasize holds and throws.
data.benefit: If you hit with an unarmed strike, you deal normal damage and can attempt to start a grapple as a free action without provoking an attack of opportunity. No initial touch attack is required. You can deal normal damage while grappling, rather than subdual damage, without suffering a penalty on your grapple check.
data.normal: Characters without this feat make a melee touch attack to grab their opponent and provoke an attack of opportunity when doing so. They also suffer a -4 penalty on their grapple checks when trying to inflict normal damage in a grapple.

data.name: Improved Heat Endurance
data.description: You can survive even in the most extreme natural heat conditions.
data.prerequisite: Base Fortitude save +6, Heat Endurance. Region: Wastes
data.benefit: You gain fire resistance 5. If you already have fire resistance, it increases by 5. You can exist comfortably in temperatures up to 160° F without having to make Fortitude saves (see Heat Dangers, page 12, Sandstorm). Your protection against heat is level 3 (see Protection against Heat, page 14, Sandstorm).

data.name: Improved Heighten Spell
data.description: You can cast a spell at any level above its own.
data.benefit: As Heighten Spell, but there is no limit to the level to which you can heighten the spell.
data.normal: Without this feat, a spell can only be heightened to a maximum of 9th level.

data.name: Improved Iaijutsu Focus
data.description: The maximum bonus damage you gain for using Iaijutsu Focus increases.
data.benefit: The extra damage you inflict when you make an Iaijutsu Focus check no longer has a maximum die cap. The progression of extra damage continues indefinitely, increasing by +1d6 for every 5 points over a result of 10 you receive on the check. For example, an Iaijutsu Focus check of 76 would inflict +14d6 points of damage.
data.normal: Without this feat, the most extra damage you can gain with an Iaijutsu Focus check is +9d6.

data.name: Improved Inertial Armor
data.description: You upgrade your Inertial Armor.
data.prerequisite: Inertial Armor, reserve power points (see text)
data.benefit: The improved field of protective energy provides a +2 armor bonus. This bonus stacks with the bonus from the Inertial Armor feat. The reserve power points requirement from Inertial Armor is increased by 2.
data.special: You can take this feat multiple times. Each time this feat is taken, it provides a +2 armor bonus. The bonus stacks with that of Inertial Armor and Improved Inertial Armor. The reserve power points requirement from Inertial Armor is increased by 2 each time the feat is taken. For example, a psychic warrior has the inertial armor feat and has taken Improved Inertial Armor 3 times. As long as she keeps 7 power points in reserve, her armor bonus is +10. If she has only 3 power points in reserve, the armor bonus is +6.

data.name: Improved Initiative
data.description: You can react more quickly than normal in a fight.
data.benefit: You get a +4 bonus on initiative checks.

data.name: Improved Ki Strike
data.description: You can strike opponents with great damage reduction.
data.benefit: Add +1 to the effective enhancement bonus of your unarmed attacks.
data.special: You can gain this feat multiple times. Its effects stack.

data.name: Improved Leadership
data.description: You are renowned for your charisma and ability to command.
data.benefit: You gain a +2 bonus to your Leadership score.

data.name: Improved Levitation
data.description: You have learned to use only part of your levitate spell-like ability at a time, allowing multiple uses with shorter durations.
data.benefit: You may use your levitate spell-like ability in 10-minute increments instead of using the entire duration at once. The number of times per day that you may use levitate at the shorter duration is equal to your caster level.If you could levitate more than once per day before taking this feat, multiply your caster level by the number of times per day you could levitate before to get the total number of shorter uses of levitate that you have available per day.

data.name: Improved Low Blow
data.description: You are especially good at using the Low Blow feat.
data.prerequisite: Dodge, Mobility, Low Blow, base attack bonus +4
data.benefit: Using the Low Blow feat does not provoke an attack of opportunity when you move into an opponent's square to perform the attack.
data.normal: A character with the Low Blow feat may enter an adjacent opponent's square to make a single attack, then return to his own square. The opponent is considered flat-footed against the attack, but the character is subject to an attack of opportunity.

data.name: Improved Low-light Vision
data.description: The range of your low-light vision is greater than normal
data.benefit: The range of your low-light vision doublet This feat does not stack with low-light vision granted by magic items or non-permanent magical effects.
data.special: This feat may be taken multiple times. Its effects stack. Remember that two doublings equals a tripling, and so on.

data.name: Improved Maneuverability
data.description: Your maneuverability in flight improves.
data.prerequisite: Fly speed 150 feet, Hover or Wingover
data.benefit: Your maneuverability improves by one category from clumsy to poor, poor to average, or average to good (see Tactical Aerial Movement, page 20 of the Dungeon Master's Guide).
data.special: You can take this feat multiple times. Each time you take the feat, your maneuverability improves by one category (but never becomes better than good).

data.name: Improved Manifestation
data.description: You can manifest psionic powers more powerful than the normal limits of manifestation.
data.prerequisite: Ability to manifest powers of the normal maximum level in at least one psionic class
data.benefit: When you select this feat, your metapsionic power point cost limit is increased by +2. For example, if you select this feat at 21st level, you could use metapsionic feats in conjunction with other powers so that you could spend a total of 22 power points on any single power. However, to manifest the power, your key ability score must be equal to or higher than the total power point cost minus 2.You must use this feat as a member of the class in which you can already manifest powers of the normal maximum level. For instance, a 5th-level psychic warrior/22nd-level psion couldn't advance her power point cost limit in psychic warrior, because she can't manifest powers of the normal maximum level for a psychic warrior. But she could advance her power point cost limit for her psion powers. Additionally, you gain 19 power points when first taking this feat.
data.normal: Without this feat, a power altered by metapsionic feats cannot cost more power points than the manifester's level minus 1 (minimum 1).
data.special: You can gain this feat multiple times. Each time this feat is gained, your power point cost limit is increased by an additional +2 and you gain a number of power points equal to your previous benefit +2.

data.name: Improved Manyshot
data.description: You can fire even more arrows as a single attack against nearby target.
data.prerequisite: Dex 19, base attack bonus +21, Manyshot, Point Blank Shot, Rapid Shot
data.benefit: As Manyshot, but the number of arrows you can fire is limited only by your base attack bonus (two arrows, plus one arrow for every 5 points of base attack bonus above +6).
data.normal: With the Manyshot feat, you are limited to a maximum of four arrows fired (when your base attack bonus is +16 or higher).
data.special: Regardless of the number of arrows you fire, you only apply precision-based damage (such as sneak attack damage or the ranger's favored enemy bonus) once. If you score a critical hit, only one of the arrows deals critical damage (your choice); all others deal normal damage.

data.name: Improved Metamagic
data.description: You can cast spells using metamagic feats more easily than normal.
data.benefit: The spell slot modifier of all your metamagic feats is reduced by one level, to a minimum of +1. For instance, you could cast a quickened spell as a spell of three levels higher rather than four levels higher. This feat has no effect on metamagic feats whose spell slot modifier is +1 or less.
data.special: You can gain this feat multiple times. The effects stack, though you can't reduce any metamagic feat's spell slot modifier to less than +1.

data.name: Improved Metapsionics
data.description: You can manifest powers using metapsionic feats more easily than normal.
data.prerequisite: Character level 21st, four metapsionic feats, Psicraft 30 ranks.
data.benefit: Metapsionic powers you manifest cost 2 power points less than normal (to a minimum of 1 power point) For instance, you could manifest a quickened power as power that costs only 4 more power points rather than more power points.This feat has no effect on metapsionic powers that inflate the cost by only 2 power points.
data.special: You can gain this feat multiple times. The effects stack, though you can't lower the cost of any metapsionic power to less than 1 power point.

data.name: Improved Mounted Archery
data.description: You can make ranged attacks from a mount almost as well as you can from the ground.
data.prerequisite: Ride 1 rank, Mounted Archery, Mounted Combat.
data.benefit: The penalty you take when using a ranged weapon if your mount is taking a double move is eliminated, and the penalty for using a ranged weapon when your mount is running is lessened from -4 to -2. You can attack at any time during your mount's move.
data.special: A fighter may select Improved Mounted Archery as one of his fighter bonus feats.

data.name: Improved Multiattack
data.description: You are particularly adept at using all your natural weapons at once.
data.prerequisite: Three or more natural weapons, Multiattack.
data.benefit: Your secondary attacks with natural weapons have no penalty on the attack roll. You still add only 1/2 your Strength bonus, if any, to damage dealt.
data.normal: Without this feat, your secondary natural attacks are made at a -5 penalty (or a -2 penalty if you have the Multiattack feat).

data.name: Improved Multiweapon Fighting
data.description: You are expert at fighting with a weapon in each of your three or more hands. You can make up to two attacks per round with each off-hand weapon.
data.prerequisite: Dex 15, three arms, base attack bonus +9, Multidexterity, Multiweapon Fighting.
data.benefit: In addition to the single extra attack you get each round with each off-hand weapon from Multiweapon Fighting, you get a second extra attack with each such weapon, albeit at a -5 penalty
data.normal: With only Multiweapon Fighting, you get only a single attack with each off-hand weapon.
data.special: This feat replaces the Improved Two-Weapon `Fighting feat for creatures with more than two arms.

data.name: Improved Natural Armor
data.description: Your skin is even tougher than that of most of your kind.
data.benefit: Your racially granted natural armor bonus increases by +1.
data.special: A character can gain this feat multiple times. Each time the character gains this feat, his natural armor improves by an additional +1.

data.name: Improved Natural Attack
data.description: The creature's natural attacks are more dangerous than its size and type would otherwise dictate.
data.benefit: Choose one of the creature's natural attack forms. The damage for this natural weapon increases by one step, as if the creature's size by one category: 1d2, 1d3, 1d4, 1d6, 1d8, 2d6, 3d6, 4d6, 6d6, 8d6, 12d6. A weapon or attack that deals 1d10 points of damage increases as follows: 1d10, 2d8, 3d8, 4d8, 6d8, 8d8, 12d8.
data.special: This feat can be taken multiple times, but each time it applies to a different natural attack.

data.name: Improved Oneiromancy
data.description: With the Improved Oneiromancy feat, you gain additional dream-related spellcasting abilities.
data.benefit: Your spell list expands to include a number of dream-related spells, regardless of what sort of caster you might be. You must still learn or prepare these spells normally; they are added to your class list, not necessarily to the list of spells you personally know.

data.name: Improved Outer Planar Heritage
data.description: Your ancestral ties to the Outer Planes manifests as an ability to deal damage with your natural attacks as if they matched the alignment of your ancestors.
data.prerequisite: Anarchic Heritage, Axiomatic Heritage, Celestial Heritage, or Fiendish Heritage.
data.benefit: Your unarmed attacks and natural weapons (but not the weapons you wield) are treated as having an alignment for the purpose of overcoming damage reduction. The alignment depends on the heritage feat you used to qualify for this feat: chaotic (Anarchic Heritage), evil (Fiendish Heritage), good (Celestial Heritage), or lawful (Axiomatic Heritage).

data.name: Improved Overrun
data.description: You are skilled at knocking down opponents.
data.benefit: When you attempt to overrun an opponent, the target cannot avoid you. You also gain +4 bonus on your Strength check to knock down your opponent.
data.normal: Without this feat, the target of an overrun can choose to avoid or block you.

data.name: Improved Paralysis
data.description: You are better at paralyzing your victims.
data.prerequisite: Undead type, paralysis special ability, Ability Focus (paralysis).
data.benefit: When your natural attacks threaten to paralyze your foe, add a +4 bonus to the save DC.

data.name: Improved Poltergeist Hand
data.description: You can move a large object at a distance when you are a ghost.
data.benefit: You can move a single object as if using the sustained force version of the telekinesis spell, except that it lasts as long as you concentrate. Your caster level is equal to your Hit Dice. A creature can resist being moved with a successful Will saving throw (DC 10 + 1/2 your HD + your Cha modifier).

data.name: Improved Power
data.description: Chose one of your domains. Through your long devotion and hours of study, you have unlocked one of the improved granted powers of that domain.
data.prerequisite: Granted power of the same domain as the selected improved granted power.
data.benefit: You may use the improved granted power of the chosen domain.
data.special: You can gain this feat multiple times. Its effects do not stack. Each time you take the feat, it applies to a new domain.

data.name: Improved Power Resistance
data.description: See Improved Spell Resistance
data.benefit: See Improved Spell Resistance

data.name: Improved Precise Shot
data.description: Your ranged attacks can ignore the effects of cover or concealment.
data.prerequisite: Dex 19, Point Blank Shot, Precise Shot, base attack bonus +11.
data.benefit: Your ranged attacks ignore the AC bonus granted to targets by anything less than total cover, and the miss chance granted to targets by anything less than total concealment. Total cover and total concealment provide their normal benefits against your ranged attacks.In addition, when you shoot or throw ranged weapons at a grappling opponent, you automatically strike at the opponent you have chosen.
data.normal: Without this feat, a character who shoots or throws a ranged weapon at a target involved in a grapple must roll randomly to see which grappling combatant the attack strikes.
data.special: An 11th-level ranger who has chosen the archery combat style s treated as having Improved Precise Shot, even if he does not have the prerequisites for it, but only when he is wearing light or no armor.

data.name: Improved Psicrystal
data.description: You can upgrade your psicrystal.
data.benefit: You can implant another personality fragment in your psicrystal. You gain the benefits of both psicrystal personalities. Your psicrystal's personality adjusts and becomes a blend between all implanted personality fragments. From now on, when determining the abilities of your psicrystal, treat your manifester level as one higher than your normal manifester level.
data.special: You can gain this feat multiple times. Each time, you implant a new personality fragment in your psicrystal, from which you derive the noted benefits, and you treat your level as one higher for the purpose of determining your psicrystal's abilities.

data.name: Improved Psionic Sidestep
data.description: You have a greater knack for avoiding attacks of opportunity.
data.prerequisite: Dex 15, Wis 15, Dodge, Mobility, Psionic Sidestep, reserve power points 9+
data.benefit: Once per round when you provoke an attack of opportunity, you can move up to 10 feet as a free action. If this movement takes you out of the attacker's reach, the attack of opportunity fails. This 10 feet of movement does not itself provoke any attacks of opportunity. If your free 10 feet of movement does not take you out of the attacker's reach, the attack does not automatically fail; however, you gain an additional +2 dodge bonus to your Armor Class for this attack; this dodge bonus stacks with the dodge bonus from the Psionic Sidestep feat.

data.name: Improved Rapid Shot
data.description: You are an expert at firing weapons with exceptional speed.
data.prerequisite: Manyshot, Point Blank Shot, Rapid Shot.
data.benefit: When using the Rapid Shot feat, you may ignore the -2 penalty on all your ranged attack rolls.
data.special: A fighter may select Improved Rapid Shot as one of his fighter bonus feats.

data.name: Improved Rapidstrike
data.description: You can make multiple attacks with a natural weapon.
data.prerequisite: Dex 9, one or more pairs of natural weapons, aberration, dragon, elemental, magical beast, or plant type, base attack bonus +15, Rapidstrike.
data.benefit: If you have a pair of natural weapons, such as two claws, two wings, or two slams, you can make two or more extra attacks with one of those weapons, the first at a -4 penalty and the second and subsequent attacks at an additional -5, but never more than four extra attacks. Creatures with multiple limbs qualify for this feat as well, so a creature with three arms and three claw attacks qualifies for this feat.
data.normal: Without this feat, you attack once with each natural weapon.
data.special: You can take this feat once for each pair of natural weapons you have. For example, a Large true dragon has one bite, two claws, two wings, and one tail attacks. The dragon can take this feat twice, once for its claws and once for its wings.

data.name: Improved Rock Hurling
data.description: Your accuracy and effectiveness with thrown rocks improves.
data.prerequisite: Str 23, Rock Hurling, size Large or larger (goliaths qualify by virtue of their powerful build racial trait).
data.benefit: You can hurl rocks weighing 40 to 50 pounds (Small objects) with a range increment of 80 feet. The rocks deal 2d6 points of damage plus your Strength modifier. Rocks have a maximum range of five range increments. Additionally, you gain a +1 bonus on attack rolls when throwing rocks.

data.name: Improved Scent
data.description: You can detect and track creatures by smell at greater distances than normal.
data.benefit: You can detect approaching enemies and sniff out hidden foes within 60 feet. For strong scents such as smoke or rotting garbage, double these ranges. Overpowering scents, such as skunk musk or troglodyte stench can be detected at triple normal range.
data.normal: Without this feat, you can detect creatures by smell only within 30 feet.

data.name: Improved Shield Bash
data.description: You can bash with a shield while retaining it shield bonus to your Armor Class.
data.benefit: When you perform a shield bash, you may still apply the shield's shield bonus to your AC.
data.normal: Without this feat, a character who performs a shield bash loses the shield's shield bonus to AC until his or her next turn.

data.name: Improved Shield Snare
data.description: You can follow up on a shield snare with deadly attacks upon your opponent.
data.prerequisite: Combat Reflexes, Improved Shield Bash, Shield Proficiency, Shield Snare.
data.benefit: When you disarm a foe with the Shield Snare feat, you can make an attack of opportunity against that opponent on the same round. Thereafter, you gain a +2 bonus on each attack roll you subsequently make against that opponent, as long as the opponent remains disarmed.
data.special: A fighter can take Improved Shield Snare as one of her bonus feats.

data.name: Improved Shieldmate
data.description: You have an outstanding ability to protect those near you with your shield.
data.benefit: As Shieldmate, except that the bonus is +2, or +3 if you are using a tower shield.
data.special: A fighter may select Improved Shieldmate as one of his fighter bonus feats.

data.name: Improved Sigil (Aesh)
data.description: You tap into your aesh power sigil to gain enhanced accuracy with your favored melee weapons.
data.benefit: Your aesh power sigil grants you a +1 insight bonus per power sigil on melee weapon damage rolls with any weapon with which you have selected the Weapon Focus feat.

data.name: Improved Sigil (Hoon)
data.description: You tap into your hoon power sigil to help survive deadly conditions.
data.benefit: Your hoon power sigil grants you a +1 insight bonus per power sigil on saving throws against death effects, on saves to avoid death by massive damage, and on Fortitude saves to avoid nonlethal damage from hot and cold environments or to resist damage from suffocation.

data.name: Improved Sigil (Krau)
data.description: You tap into your krau power sigil to augment the energy of your magical utterances.
data.benefit: Choose a number of spells with verbal components that you can cast equal to the number of power sigils you have. When you cast one of the chosen spells, the spell's effective level is increased by 1 (as if affected by the Heighten Spell feat, but with no change to the spell's casting time or spell slot). All effects dependent on spell level are calculated according to the heightened level.Whenever you gain the ability to cast a new level of spells, you can reassign the spells you've chosen to be enhanced by this feat. For example, a wizard who reaches 3rd level and gains the ability to cast 2nd-level spells may reassign the spells affected by this feat.
data.special: If you cast one of your chosen spells using the Silent Spell feat, this feat has no effect.

data.name: Improved Sigil (Naen)
data.description: You tap into your naen power sigil to see through illusions and resist language-based effects.
data.benefit: Your naen power sigil grants you a +1 insight bonus per power sigil on saving throws against illusions and against language-dependent effects.

data.name: Improved Sigil (Uur)
data.description: You tap into your uur power sigil to gain enhanced accuracy with ranged weapons.
data.benefit: Your uur power sigil grants you a +1 insight bonus per power sigil on ranged weapon damage rolls whenever your target is denied its Dexterity bonus to Armor Class.

data.name: Improved Sigil (Vaul)
data.description: You tap into your vaul power sigil to resist mental effects.
data.benefit: Your vaul power sigil grants you a +1 insight bonus per power sigil on saving throws against mind-affecting spells and abilities.

data.name: Improved Skirmish
data.description: With a few extra steps, you gain even greater benefits from your skirmishing combat style.
data.benefit: If you move at least 20 feet away from where you were at the start of your turn, your skirmish damage increases by 2d6 and your competence bonus to AC from skirmish improves by 2.
data.normal: A scout's bonus damage and AC bonus apply if she moves at least 10 feet away from where she was at the start of her turn.
data.special: A scout can select Improved Skirmish as one of her scout bonus feats.

data.name: Improved Skirmish (Epic)
data.description: Your combat mobility improves.
data.benefit: If the bonus to AC granted when you use your skirmish ability is equal to or higher than the number of dice of extra damage granted by your skirmish ability, your extra damage on skirmish attacks increases by 1d6.If the bonus to AC granted by your skirmish ability is less than the number of dice of extra damage granted by your skirmish ability, the bonus to AC when skirmishing increases by 1 instead.
data.special: This feat may be taken multiple times. Its effects stack.

data.name: Improved Smiting
data.description: Your smite attacks deal more damage to specific foes, and can damage creatures with alignment-based damage reduction.
data.benefit: Whenever you make a smite attack, your attack overcomes damage reduction as if had an alignment, and you deal an extra +1d6 points of damage to targets of a specific alignment.If the smite attack has an alignment associated with it, it deals its extra damage to foes of that alignment and it is treated as having the opposite alignment for overcoming damage reduction. For example, a paladin's smite evil attacks are treated as having the good alignment and deal +1d6 damage to evil targets, while a blackguard's smite good attacks are treated as having the evil alignment and deal +1d6 damage to good targets.If the smite attack has no alignment associated with it, you must choose an alignment component (chaotic, evil, good, or lawful) when you select the feat. Your smite attacks overcome damage reduction as if they had that alignment, and deal +1d6 points of damage to foes of the opposite alignment.For example, a lawful neutral cleric of St. Cuthbert with the Destruction domain who selected this feat must choose for his smite attacks to be lawfully aligned (and these attacks would deal +1d6 points of damage to chaotic targets). A lawful evil cleric of Hextor with the Destruction domain could choose to have his smite attacks be lawfully or evilly aligned (and these attacks would deal +1d6 points of damage to chaotic targets or to good targets, respectively).You can't choose an alignment component that isn't part of your alignment, and once this choice is made, it can never be changed. If you later change alignment so that the chosen alignment component is no longer part of your alignment, you lose the benefits of this feat.
data.special: If you have the smite ability from more than one class, the effect of the feat applies to all your smite abilities, and it is possible to select different alignments for each (as long as the alignments chosen are legal selections). For example, a paladin/cleric with the Destruction domain must choose good for his smite good ability, but could choose law for his smite domain power.

data.name: Improved Snatch
data.description: You can make snatch attacks against bigger opponents than other creatures can.
data.benefit: As the Snatch feat, except that you can grab a creature two size categories smaller than you with your bite or claw attack.

data.name: Improved Snatch Spell
data.description: When you take over a spell from another spellcaster, you gain more control over its effect.
data.prerequisite: Knowledge (arcana) 40 ranks, Spellcraft 40 ranks, Epic Skill Focus (Spellcraft), snatch spell class ability.
data.benefit: When you use your seize concentration or snatch spell ability, you can enhance and control the spell's effect as though you had cast it yourself. You can substitute your own caster level for the original caster, level if you wish, and you can change any other variables determined by the original caster.

data.name: Improved Sneak Attack
data.description: Your sneak attacks are more deadly than normal.
data.benefit: Add +1d6 to your sneak attack damage.
data.special: This feat may be taken multiple times. Its effects stack.

data.name: Improved Speed
data.description: You are faster than others of your kind.
data.benefit: Your fly speed (if you have one) improves by 20 feet. All other speeds you have improve by 10 feet.

data.name: Improved Spell Capacity
data.description: You can prepare spells that exceed the normal limits of spellcasting.
data.prerequisite: Ability to cast spells of the normal maximum spell level in at least one spellcasting class.
data.benefit: When you select this feat, you gain one spell slot per day of any level up to one level higher than the highest level spell you can already cast in a particular class. For example, if you select this feat as a 21st-level wizard, you would gain one wizard spell slot of any spell level up to 10th. You must still have the requisite ability score (10 + spell level) to cast any spell stored in this slot. If you have a high enough ability modifier to gain one or more bonus spells for this spell level, you also gain the bonus spells for this spell level.You must use the spell slot as a member of the class in which you can already cast spells of the normal maximum spell level. For instance, a 5th-level ranger/22nd-level sorcerer couldn't add a ranger spell slot, because he can't cast spells of the normal maximum spell level for rangers. He must add the spell slot to his sorcerer spells.
data.special: You can gain this feat multiple times.

data.name: Improved Spell Resistance
data.description: Your innate resistance to magical effects increases.
data.prerequisite: Must have spell resistance from a feat, class feature, or other permanent effect
data.benefit: Your spell resistance increases by +2.
data.special: You can gain this feat multiple times. Its effects stack.

data.name: Improved Spellpool Access
data.description: You can use your spellpool access to call spells of greater than normal power.
data.prerequisite: Knowledge (arcana) 30 ranks, Spellcraft 30 ranks, ability to call spells from a spellpool.
data.benefit: The chance of a desired spell being available in the spellpool in any given 24-hour period is equal to 90% minus 5% per spell level (minimum 5% for spells of 17th level or higher).
data.normal: The chance of a spell being available is equal to 65% minus 5% per spell level (minimum 5% for spells of 12th level or higher).
data.special: This feat should be considered part of the epic bonus feat list for any class that grants its members spellpool access.

data.name: Improved Spit
data.description: You can spit farther than normal.
data.prerequisite: Precise Shot and Spit Venom, spit attack, or spittle attack.
data.benefit: The range of your spit attack doubles.
data.special: You may take Improved Spit multiple times. Each time you take the feat, the range of your spit attack increases by an amount equal to its original range.

data.name: Improved Stunning Fist
data.description: Your stunning fist attack becomes more formidable.
data.prerequisite: Dex 19, Wis 19, Improved Unarmed Strike, Stunning Fist
data.benefit: Add +2 to the DC of your stunning attack
data.special: This feat may be taken multiple times. Its effects stack.

data.name: Improved Sudden Strike
data.description: Your ability to strike unaware foes improves.
data.benefit: Add 1d6 to your sudden strike damage.
data.special: This feat may be taken multiple times. Its effects stack.

data.name: Improved Sunder
data.description: You are skilled at attacking your opponents' weapons and shields, as well as other objects.
data.benefit: When you strike at an object help or carried by an opponent ( such as a weapon or shield), you do not provoke an attack of opportunity (see Sundering).You also gain a +4 bonus on any attack roll made to attack an object held or carried by another character.
data.normal: Without this feat, you provoke an attack of opportunity when you strike at an object held or carried by another.

data.name: Improved Swimming
data.description: You can swim faster than you normally could.
data.benefit: You can swim half your speed as a move action or your speed as a full-round action.
data.normal: You swim at one-quarter your speed as a move action or at half your speed as a full-round action.

data.name: Improved Toughness
data.description: You are significantly tougher than normal.
data.benefit: You gain a number of hit points equal to your current Hit Dice. Each time you gain a HD (such as by gaining a level), you gain 1 additional hit point. If you lose a HD (such as by losing a level), you lose 1 hit point permanently.
data.special: A fighter may select Improved Toughness as one of his fighter bonus feats.

data.name: Improved Trip
data.description: You are trained not only in tripping opponents but in following through with an attack.
data.benefit: You do not provoke an attack of opportunity when you attempt to trip an opponent while you are unarmed. You also gain a +4 bonus on your Strength check to trip your opponent.If you trip an opponent in melee combat, you immediately get a melee attack against that opponent as if you hadn't used your attack for the trip attempt. For example, at 11th level, Tordek gets three attacks at bonuses of +11, +6, and +1. In the current round, he attempts to trip his opponent. His first attempt fails (using up his first attack). His second attempt succeeds, and he immediately makes a melee attack against his opponent with a bonus of +6. Finally, he takes his last attack at a bonus of +1.
data.normal: Without this feat, you provoke an attack of opportunity when you attempt to trip an opponent while you unarmed. See Trip.
data.special: At 6th level, a monk may select Improved Trip bonus feat, even if she does not have the prerequisites.

data.name: Improved Turn Resistance
data.description: You have a better than normal chance to resist turning.
data.benefit: You are less easily affected by clerics or paladins than you normally would be (see Turn or Rebuke Undead, page 159 of the Player's Handbook). When resolving a turn, rebuke, command, or bolster attempt, add +4 to your character level (monster Hit Dice plus class levels) to determine your Hit Dice for turn, rebuke, command, and bolster attempts. For example, a 4 HD wight with this feat is treated as an 8 HD undead for the purpose of turn, rebuke, command, and bolster attempts, even though it is a 4 HD creature for any other purpose. A vampire that already has +4 turn resistance adds an additional +4 with this feat, for a total of +8.

data.name: Improved Turn Resistance (GW)
data.description: You are better able to resist the channeling of positive or negative energy by clerics and similar classes.
data.benefit: You gain turn resistance +2. If you already have turn resistance, your turn resistance increases by 2.
data.special: You may take this feat multiple times. Its effects stack. Each time you take it, your turn resistance increases by 2

data.name: Improved Turning
data.description: Your turning or rebuking attempts are more powerful than normal.
data.prerequisite: Ability to turn or rebuke undead.
data.benefit: You turn or rebuke creatures as if you were one level higher than your are in the class that grants you're the ability.

data.name: Improved Two-weapon Defense
data.description: You gain a significant defensive advantage while fighting with two weapons.
data.prerequisite: Dex 17, Two-Weapon Defense, Two-Weapon Fighting, base attack bonus +6.
data.benefit: When wielding two weapons (not including natural weapons or unarmed strikes), you gain a +2 shield bonus to your Armor Class.When you are fighting defensively or using the total defense action, this shield bonus increases to +4.
data.special: A fighter may select Improved Two-Weapon Defense as one of his fighter bonus feats.

data.name: Improved Two-Weapon Fighting
data.description: You are an expert in fighting two-handed.
data.prerequisite: Dex 17, Two-Weapon Fighting, base attack bonus +9
data.benefit: In addition to the standard single extra attack you get with an off-hand weapon, you get a second attack with the off-hand weapon, albeit at a -5 penalty (see Two-Weapon Fighting).
data.special: A 6th-level ranger who has chosen the two-weapon combat style is treated as having Improved Two-Weapon Fighting, even if he does not the prerequisites for it, but only he is when wearing light armor or no armor.

data.name: Improved Unarmed Strike
data.description: You are skilled at fighting while unarmed.
data.benefit: You are considered to be armed even when unarmed - that is, armed opponents do not get attacks of opportunity when you attack them while unarmed. However, you still get an opportunity attack against any opponent who makes an unarmed attack on you.In addition, your unarmed strikes can deal lethal or nonlethal damage, at your option.
data.normal: Without this feat, you are considered unarmed when attacking with an unarmed strike, and you can deal only nonlethal damage with such an attack.
data.special: A monk fighting unarmed automatically gains the benefit of this feat.

data.name: Improved Weapon Familiarity
data.description: You are familiar with all exotic weapons common to your people.
data.benefit: You can treat all the exotic weapons associated with your race as martial weapons rather than as exotic weapons. A weapon is treated as being associated with a race if the race's name appears as part of the weapons name, such as the elven thinblade or the dwarven urgrosh.
data.normal: Without this feat, you must select the Exotic Weapon Proficiency feat (or have the appropriate weapon familiarity as a racial trait) to eliminate the non-proficiency penalty you take when wielding an exotic weapon associated with your race.
data.special: A fighter may select Improved Weapon Familiarity as one of his fighter bonus feats.

data.name: Improved Web
data.description: You gain additional utility from your webs.
data.prerequisite: Ability to create webs as an extraordinary ability at least twice per day.
data.benefit: Add +2 to the DC to escape or burst your webs. You may take a full-round action to attack with your webs and if you do so, you may attack one additional target per point of Dexterity bonus you have. No such target may be more than 10 feet from another target. Using this feat requires two of the daily uses of your web ability

data.name: Improved Whirlwind Attack
data.description: You can strike at nearby opponents in a blindingly fast spinning assault and follow it up with extra attacks.
data.prerequisite: Dex 13, Int 13, Combat Expertise, Dodge, Mobility, Spring Attack, Whirlwind Attack, base attack bonus +4.
data.benefit: As part of a whirlwind attack full-attack action, you can make all of your additional attacks based on your base attack bonus in addition to your normal attacks granted by the feat. For example, a 12th-level fighter with Improved Whirlwind Attack can make all his normal Whirlwind Attacks (each at +12 plus Strength and other modifiers) and then make the two additional attacks he gains from having a high base attack bonus (at +7 and +2 plus Strength and other modifiers). He does not get to make these additional attacks against every opponent; he only gets the two attacks at the end of his whirlwind.
data.special: A fighter may select Improved Whirlwind Attack as one of his fighter bonus feats.

data.name: Improvised Trip Attack
data.description: You can trip foes with thrown weapons.
data.prerequisite: Base attack bonus +3 or better, Improvised Weapon Proficiency or the brawler's improvised weapon ability
data.benefit: When using a weapon or improvised weapon, you can attempt to trip foes at range by throwing the weapon at the foe. When you attempt to do so, make a ranged touch attack with the weapon. If you hit, you and your foe make an opposed Strength check, following the normal rules for trip attempts. Your size does not count toward your Strength check.The size of the weapon used might give you a bonus or a penalty. If the weapon is of a weapon size category equal to the creature size category of the foe you wish to trip, you suffer no penalty and gain no bonus. For each size category smaller the weapon is, you suffer a -4 penalty. For each size category larger the weapon is, you gain a +4 bonus. For instance, throwing a short sword (Small) at a halfling would give you no bonus or penalty. Throwing the same weapon at a human would cause you to suffer a -4 penalty, and throwing it at an ogre would cause you to suffer a -8 penalty.

data.name: Improvised Weapon Proficiency
data.description: You are skilled at using any object as a weapon.
