You add your favored enemy bonus for that type of creature to saving throws against the standard extraordinary, spell-like, and supernatural abilities of your favored enemy. You do not gain this bonus against other attacks by the creature. For example, a ranger with undead as a favored enemy would gain his favored enemy bonus on saves against a vampire's domination ability but not to saves against the poison on its dagger or any saves against spells it cast from class levels. This saving throw bonus applies to all secondary effects of the favored enemy's attacks, so the ranger would also get his favored enemy bonus on the Fortitude save to remove a negative level caused by the vampire's energy drain ability.

data.name: Witchlight
data.description: You can create witchlight, a harmless faint light, on yourself or an object.
data.benefit: As a standard action, you can cause some or all of your body or an unattended object up to Medium-size that you touch to glow with witchlight, a harmless supernatural flame resembling faerie fire. Witchlight sheds light as a candle and may be pale red, orange, green, blue, or violet. Maintaining witchlight on an object as long as it is within 100 feet of you. This requires concentration, and you can maintain its effect on an object as long as it is within 100 feet of you. This is a supernatural ability.You can use witchlight to outline a creature (such as one under the effects of blur, invisibility and so on), but doing so requires a touch attack (which draws an attack of opportunity from the defender).

data.name: Wolf Berserker
data.description: You have studied the fighting style of the wolf and employ its tactics in combat.
data.prerequisite: Ability to rage, membership in the Wolf berserker lodge (see Berserker Lodges). Region: Rashemen
data.benefit: You gain a +4 bonus on trip attacks and on checks to resist an opponent's trip attacks.
data.special: Characters with this feat may choose the Improved Trip feat even if they don't meet the other prerequisites for Improved Trip.

data.name: Wolf's Bite
data.description: You temporarily master special attacks.
data.benefit: You can spend one daily use of ki power to gain access to Improved Disarm, Improved Feint, and Improved Trip for 1 round.

data.name: Wolfpack
data.description: You can gain an extra advantage when you and your allies can gang up on a foe.
data.prerequisite: Dex 15, Dodge, Mobility, Spring Attack, base attack bonus +6.
data.benefit: You can use the following maneuvers with this feat.

data.name: Wolverine's Rage
data.description: You can fly into a berserk rage when injured.
data.benefit: If you have taken damage during the last round, you may spend a wild shape as a free action on your turn to enter a rage. While in this rage, you gain a +2 bonus to Strength, a +2 bonus to Constitution, and take a -2 penalty to AC. This rage lasts for 5 rounds and cannot be ended voluntarily.

data.name: Woodland Archer
data.description: You have honed your archery ability in the wilds of the forest.
data.prerequisite: Point Blank Shot, base attack bonus +6.
data.benefit: The Woodland Archer feat enables the use of three tactical maneuvers.

data.name: Woodwise
data.description: You are trained in fighting in woodlands and know how to use the terrain to best advantage. Region: Elf (the Misty Vale), star elf (Sildëyuir), or volodni (the Forest of Lethyr)
data.benefit: You ignore hampered movement penalties from naturally occurring moderate or heavy undergrowth. (You are still hampered by magically enhanced brush and plants.) When fighting in wooded areas, you gain a +1 dodge bonus to AC.
data.normal: A character without this feat is reduced to three-quarters normal speed in moderate underbrush and half normal speed in heavy underbrush, and gains no defensive bonus for fighting in woodlands.

data.name: Word Given Form
data.description: You have mastered the martial arts style of "Word Given Form"
data.prerequisite: Truespeak 12 ranks, Tumble 12 ranks, Combat Expertise, Dodge, Improved Unarmed Strike, Mobility, Spring Attack.
data.benefit: When using the Dodge feat against an opponent in addition to the +1 dodge bonus to AC, you also gain total concealment (50% miss chance) from that opponent as your form blurs and shifts.

data.name: Words of Creation
data.description: You have learned a few of the words that were spoken to create the world.
data.prerequisite: Int 15, Cha 15, base Will save bonus +5.
data.benefit: You can use the Words of Creation to enhance bardic music, to help cast good spells and create good magic items, and to enhance the process of creation (see Words of Creation).
data.normal: A nonevil creature that tries to utter the Words of Creation without learning them properly is affected as by a feeblemind spell, while an evil character is struck dead. Fortunately, it is impossible to make someone speak the Words of Creation against his will, because their pronunciation is so exacting.

data.name: Words of Draconic Power
data.description: You tap into the great tradition of draconic magic to enhance the words of your allies.
data.benefit: You gain access to ceremonies based on your ranks in Knowledge (religion). Each ceremony requires 5 minutes, but their forms vary as described below. Each ceremony can affect up to six individuals, and the effects last for 24 hours. Ancient Tongue (4 ranks): Using a tindertwig, you burn dragon runes onto a piece of bread (one per participant) and place it on the participants tongue, thereby sharing your knowledge of the ancient language of dragons. Each participant gains the ability to speak and understand the Draconic language. Potent Words (8 ranks): You write mystic incantations on a flask or alchemist's fire (one per participant), making it safe to consume. Each participant then drinks the contents of the flask. When a participant casts a language-dependent spell, his caster level is increased by 1. Participants also gain a +1 bonus on saves against language-dependent spells. Arcane Symbols (14 ranks): You scribe a single draconic rune on a pearl worth at least 1,000 gp (one per participant), which is consumed by the participant. At any one time during the next 24 hours, the participant can choose to activate the ceremony's power as an immediate action. This has one of two effects, chosen by the participant. If the character is being subjected to a power word spell, he can treat his current hit points as twice their actual number for the purpose of determining the effect of the spell. For example, a character with 55 hit points who activated this ability in response to a power word kill spell would be Unaffected by the spell (since he would effectively have 101 hit points.)If the character is capable of casting a power word spell (that is, he either has such a spell prepared, or he knows one and has a spell slot available to cast it) he can cast that power word spell with no additional action required. Doing this expends the prepared spell or spell slot as normal.

data.name: World Weary
data.description: Your crushed spirit makes you difficult to scare or intimidate. Region: Ablissa, Bandit Kingdoms, Boner March, Geoff, Iuz, North Kingdom, Pomarj, Rovers of the Barrens, Scarlet Brotherhood, Sea Princes, Shield Lands, Sterich, Tenh (Oerth)
data.benefit: You receive a +4 competence bonus on saves against fear. Treat fear conditions as one rank lower, so an effect the would make you panicked instead makes you frightened, while an effect that makes you frightened merely leaves you shaken. Effects that make you shaken affect you as normal.

data.name: Wormbound
data.description: You have taken a vile green Kyuss worm into your own body and bound yourself to its power.
data.prerequisite: Living creature, host a living Kyuss worm.
data.benefit: Successfully hosting a Kyuss worm requires a special binding ritual. The ritual requires 4 hours of uninterrupted meditation, 500 gp in special materials, and a living Kyuss worm. At the end of the ceremony, you must succeed at a DC 15 Fortitude saving throw or die. If you fail the saving throw, you rise as a spawn of Kyuss within 1d6+4 rounds. If you succeed at the saving throw, you can add the following spells to your class spell list:

data.name: Wounding Attack
data.description: Your vicious attacks wound your foe.
data.benefit: To use this feat, you must expend your psionic focus (see Concentration skill description). You can make an attack with such vicious force that you wound your opponent. A wound deals 1 point of Constitution damage to your foe in addition to the usual damage dealt.You must decide whether or not to use this feat prior to making an attack. If your attack misses, you still expend your psionic focus.

data.name: Wounding Spell
data.description: Because you have studied the cruel arts of the Athalantan magelords of old, you know how to cast spells that cause terrible, bleeding wounds.
data.benefit: When affected by this feat, a spell that deals damage to a creature also inflicts a bleeding wound that does not heal normally. On each subsequent round, the victim loses 1 hit point at the beginning of your turn. The continuing hit point loss can be stopped with a Heal check (DC equal to the spell's save DC, or the save DC it would otherwise have in the case of a spell with no save), a cure spell, or a heal spell.You cannot apply this feat to a spell that does not deal damage (such as charm person or baleful polymorph).A wounding spell occupies a spell slot two levels higher than the spell's actual level.

data.name: Wyrmgrafter
data.description: You can apply draconic grafts to other living creatures or to yourself.
data.benefit: You can create draconic grafts and apply them to other living creatures or to yourself Creating a draconic graft takes one day for each 1,000 gp in its price. To create a graft, you must spend 1/25 of the graft's price in XP and use up raw materials costing half this price.Draconic grafts are described beginning on page 126, Races of the Dragon

data.name: Xaniqos School
data.description: You have learned the fighting style of House Xaniqos, which favors aggressive crossbow maneuvers.
data.prerequisite: Dex 13, Dodge, Mobility, Point Blank Shot, Shot on the Run, base attack bonus +6
data.benefit: If you have moved at least 10 feet toward your target since the start of your turn, your crossbow attacks deal an extra 1d6 points of damage against that target in this round. This feat's benefit doesn't apply when you are mounted.In addition, reloading your crossbow does not provoke attacks of opportunity.

data.name: Xeph Burst Extra
data.description: You can use your burst racial trait more often.
data.benefit: You can use the burst ability granted by your xeph heritage a total of three times per day.
data.normal: A xeph's burst ability can be used once per day.
data.special: You can gain this feat multiple times. Each time you take the feat, you gain an additional two daily uses of your burst ability.

data.name: Xeph Celerity
data.description: You can use your burst racial trait to gain an extra attack.
data.benefit: As a swift action, you can expend one or more of your daily uses of burst granted by your xeph heritage to gain one extra attack foci round, instead of gaining an increase to your speed. While enjoying your round of celerity, you make one extra attack each round, using your highest base attack bonus, when you take a full attack action.

data.name: Yondalla's Sense
data.description: You display a shrewd perception of danger. Other halflings say the blessing of Yondalla is upon you.
data.benefit: You add your Wisdom bonus on initiative checks.

data.name: Zagyg's Favor
data.description: It's not that you're insane, it's just that "normal" people don't understand the world the way you do. Either way, you're difficult to dominate effectively. Region: Greyhawk (Oerth)
data.benefit: You receive a +4 bonus on Will saves against compulsion effects (confusion, dominate person, insanity, and so on).

data.name: Zen Archery
data.description: Your intuition guides your hand when you use a ranged weapon.
data.benefit: You can use your Wisdom modifier instead of your Dexterity modifier when making a ranged attack roll.

data.name: Zen Fortitude
data.description: Your intuitive control of your body allows you to withstand physical hardships.
data.benefit: Add your Wisdom modifier (instead of your Constitution modifier) on Fortitude saves. Using this feat does not expend a daily use of your ki power. Its effects are always active.

data.name: Zone of Animation
data.description: You can channel negative energy to animate undead.
data.prerequisite: Cha 25, Undead Mastery, ability to rebuke or command undead.
data.benefit: You can use a rebuke or command undead attempt to animate corpses within range of your rebuke or command attempt. You animate a total number of HD of undead equal to the number of undead that would be commanded by your result (though you can't animate more undead than there are available corpses within range). You can't animate more undead with any single attempt than the maximum number you can command (including any undead already under your command). These undead are automatically under your command, though your normal limit of commanded undead still applies.If the corpses are relatively fresh, the animated undead are zombies. Otherwise, they are skeletons.
